Japanese: ３月２２日　午後　５時２４分 成歩堂法律事務所
English: March 22, 5:24 PM Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: 真宵さま‥‥真宵さまああ‥‥ ううう‥‥うっ
English: Mystic Maya... M-Mystic Mayaaa... *sniffle, sniffle*

Japanese: ごめん‥‥春美ちゃん‥‥
English: There, there, Pearls...

Japanese: わたくし‥‥もうダメです‥‥ ‥‥あああああん‥‥
English: I... I can't take it... anymore... ...Waaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa...

Japanese: だいじょうぶだよ！ まだ、チャンスはある。
English: L-Look! It'll be alright! Everything may still work out.

Japanese: 誘拐犯の要求は、 “無罪判決”のハズだ。
English: The condition was that we had to get a "not guilty" verdict.

Japanese: うかつに真宵ちゃんに 手は出せない。
English: And so far, the kidnapper has kept his word and hasn't hurt Maya.

Japanese: ‥‥まだオートロさんは、有罪に なったわけじゃないんだからね！
English: And he won't because Mr. Engarde hasn't been given a "guilty" sentence yet!

Japanese: うっ‥‥うう‥‥
English: Unngg... Nnmff... *sniffle, sniffle*

Japanese: ‥‥泣いてるヒマはないよ。 今は、行動しないと！
English: Cheer up! We don't have time to stand around crying! We have to get going!

Japanese: そ‥‥そうですよね。
English: Y-You're right...

Japanese: 真宵さまは‥‥もっと おツラいんですから！
English: Mystic Maya is in much more pain than I am!

Japanese: そうだよ！　じゃあ‥‥
English: Yes, that's right. So...

Japanese: おッ！　あんたたちッ！ まだいたッスね！
English: Hey! You guys! Glad I caught you, pal!

Japanese: お‥‥おひげのけいじさん‥‥
English: M-Mr. Scruffy Detective...

Japanese: （春美ちゃんにとって、 イトノコ刑事は‥‥）
English: (...Oh boy. Looks like Detective Gumshoe...)

Japanese: （“おひげの　けいじ”という 名前のオジさんらしいな）
English: (has been dubbed "Mr. Scruffy Detective" in Pearls' book now...)

Japanese: 男・糸鋸　圭介。 ハナシがあって来たッス！
English: It's just plain ol' "Mr. Dick Gumshoe" now, and I came to talk to you, pal!

Japanese: （いそがしいんだけど‥‥）
English: (But we're kind of busy right now...)

Japanese: ‥‥おッ。ちょっと しおれてるッスね！
English: Hey, it's wilting a little!

Japanese: 自分が‥‥自分が、 おいしい水をおごるッス！
English: I'll... I'll give it some delicious water, pal!

Japanese: い、いや。今日は もう、あげましたから‥‥
English: Ah, it's OK... I already did that...

Japanese: ‥‥じょうろ、じょうろ‥‥
English: ...The watering can... Where are you, Mr. Watering Can...?

Japanese: （‥‥ぼくのゴキゲンをとって どうするつもりだよ‥‥）
English: (Did he just call the watering can, "Mr."?)

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本が あざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: あ。何かホーリツをお調べに なるのですか！
English: Oh! Do you need to look something up in one of these law books, Mr. Nick!?

Japanese: ど、どの本が！ どの本がヒツヨウなのですかッ！
English: Wh-Which book is it!? Which book do you need!?

Japanese: ううう‥‥漢字が読めないし、 背もとどきません‥‥
English: Umm... I can't read those hard books, and... um... I can't reach them either...

Japanese: じ、自分なら！　漢字も読めるし 背もとどくッス！
English: B-But I can read AND I'm tall, pal!

Japanese: は、春美ちゃんと 張り合わないでくださいよ。
English: P-Please stop competing with an 8 year old... It's disturbing...

Japanese: 窓の向かいがわには、 巨大なビルがそびえている。
English: There is a giant building just outside the window.

Japanese: “ホテル・バンドー” ゴウカな高級ホテルだ。
English: It's the Gatewater Hotel, a high-class, luxury hotel.

Japanese: ちなみに、事件があったホテルは 最近、街の中心に完成した、
English: Actually, the crime happened at the recently completed hotel in the center of town,

Japanese: ホテル・バンドー・インペリアル。
English: the Gatewater Imperial Hotel.

Japanese: ‥‥いったい、どこまで デカくなっていくのやら‥‥。
English: ...Just how far are they going to expand their empire...?

Japanese: ぼくのデスクだ。
English: My desk.

Japanese: あまり座る機会がないため、 いやにキチンとかたづいている。
English: Since I don't have time to sit, it's unusually clean...

Japanese: あ。わたくしがゆうべ、 さらにキチンとかたづけました！
English: Oh! I cleaned it up even more for you last night!

Japanese: じ、自分が‥‥さらに ゾーキンがけしとくッス！
English: B-But I can get it even more cleaner, pal!

Japanese: きゃあ！　それは おトイレ用の‥‥！
English: Aaah! That's... That's for the toilet!!

Japanese: （‥‥おおさわぎだな‥‥）
English: (What a mess. Literally...)

Japanese: これは‥‥とのさまん・ほい ‥‥ですね。
English: This is the... uh... Nickel Samurai, right?

Japanese: う、うん。《ほい》だね。
English: Y-Yeah, that's right.

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Mr. Nick!

Japanese: 真宵さまの《ほい》に なってくださいね！
English: Please take care of Mystic Maya, and be her "Nickel Samurai", alright!?

Japanese: これから、どうするんですか？
English: So, what are you going to do from now on?

Japanese: え？
English: Whadaya mean, pal?

Japanese: クビになったんですよね、刑事。 ‥‥新しいシゴトは？
English: Well, you've been fired, right? So, do you have a new job lined up yet?

Japanese: それッス！　じ、自分は‥‥ どうすればいいッスか！
English: Oh, that! A-Ah... What am I supposed to do now, pal!?

Japanese: 次の給料日まで 思いっきり、無収入ッス！
English: I... I don't have anything coming in at all until my next payday!

Japanese: な、ナニ言ってるんですか！
English: Wh-What are you talking about!?

Japanese: 次の給料日なんて 永久に来ませんよ！
English: You don't have another payday!

Japanese: とりあえず、アンタのところで はたらくッス！
English: I guess that means I'm just gonna half to work here at your place, pal!

Japanese: え‥‥ええッ！
English: S-SAY WHAT!?

Japanese: 明日までに、王都楼のムジツを 証明するものを探すッスよね？
English: You'll be searching for things that will prove Mr. Engarde's innocence all day, right?

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥そうですけど‥‥
English: W-Well... yeah...

Japanese: やるッス！ 自分も手伝うッス！
English: So yeah! I'm going to help you, pal!

Japanese: 捜査はモチロンのこと、 ルス番だってカンペキッス！
English: I've got lots of experience in investigating, and watching over people's places!

Japanese: あと、カンタンな 料理も、おまかせッス！
English: And I'm great at making really simple meals, pal! I'll take care of it all!

Japanese: おねがいしましょうよ！ なるほどくん。
English: Come on, Mr. Nick! Let's let Mr. Scruffy Detective take care of things!

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥。 ちなみに、得意メニューは？
English: A-Ah... OK... By the way, what's your best dish?

Japanese: ソーメン、ッス。
English: Instant noodles, pal.

Japanese: （‥‥なぜ、ぼくのまわりは めん類好きが集まるんだろう）
English: (Why am I surrounded by people who only eat cheap, unhealthy foods...?)

Japanese: ‥‥自分も、あんな御剣検事は はじめて見たッス‥‥
English: ...That was the first time I've ever seen Mr. Edgeworth act like that...

Japanese: “証人が自殺しても かまわない”なんて‥‥
English: Never thought he'd say some- thing like, he didn't care if Ms. Andrews killed herself...

Japanese: そ、そんなコトを！ なんて、ヒドい‥‥
English: H-He said that!? That's horrible...

Japanese: でも、そのおかげで 真実がわかったッス。
English: But because of him doing that, we got the truth finally.
...The truth...

Japanese: （最後の霧緒さんの証言‥‥ あれは、真実だったのだろうか）
English: (Ms. Andrews' last testimony; I wonder if that was the truth...?)

Japanese: たしかに、証言自体に おかしなところはなかった。
English: I'll give you that there was nothing strange in her testimony itself.

Japanese: しかし‥‥根本的に ナットクできない点があります。
English: But... I still think there is something fundamentally wrong with the whole thing.

Japanese: なんのコト‥‥ッスか？
English: You mean about "that" thing, pal?

Japanese: なぜそこまでして、王都楼さんに 罪を着せようとしたか‥‥
English: Why would she want to -- no, I mean, almost "need" to frame Mr. Engarde...

Japanese: その理由ですよ。 そんなもの、どこにもない！
English: I couldn't figure that out from anything she said all day!

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ‥‥あの証言は‥‥ ウソだった、と‥‥？
English: Then... Then you're saying that testimony... was a lie...?

Japanese: ‥‥何かまだ、ウラがあるような 気がするんです。
English: Not a lie, per se... It just feels like there's more here than meets the eye.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣なら、それぐらいの シナリオは作るでしょう。
English: Or that's what Edgeworth would like us to believe...

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥きたないコトを！
English: Th-That's such a dirty trick!

Japanese: 女の検事さんのほうが、 まだマシではないですか！
English: Even that woman prosecutor was better than that!

Japanese: （狩魔　冥‥‥）
English: (Franziska von Karma...)

Japanese: そういえば、彼女‥‥ 狩魔検事はどうなりました？
English: Speaking of Ms. von Karma, do you have any more information on her condition?

Japanese: たしか‥‥ “狙撃された”って！
English: Wasn't she shot this morning!?

Japanese: 今朝、法廷に向かう途中、 ピストルで撃たれたッス。
English: Ms. von Karma was shot today on the way to the trial by a pistol, pal.

Japanese: ぐあいは‥‥どうなんですか！
English: B-But she's going to be fine, right?

Japanese: 御剣は“イノチに別状はない” って言ってましたけど‥‥
English: I mean, Edgeworth said she was in "stable condition", but...

Japanese: ‥‥それは、まちがいないッス。 撃たれたのは、肩ッスから。
English: Well, she was shot in the shoulder, so she's OK and still hanging in there.

Japanese: 今は、弾丸の摘出手術も終わって、 病院で寝てるはずッス。
English: They should be done taking the bullet out, so she's probably resting at the hospital.

Japanese: ‥‥どこの病院ですか？
English: Which one...?

Japanese: お、なんスか。 ‥‥行くッスか？　おみまい。
English: What? Are you going to visit her, pal?

Japanese: い、いや‥‥まあ、 気が向いたら‥‥。
English: N-No... Well, I was kind of thinking about it...

Japanese: アンタ！ いいトコあるッスねー！
English: Hey! You've actually got a heart, don't you!?

Japanese: 法廷に出られなくて、死ぬほど くやしがっていたッスから‥‥
English: She looked like she was being tortured to death not being able to go to the trial today,

Japanese: 思いっきり、たたかれて あげるといいッス！　ムチで。
English: so maybe it'd be good for her if you went and let her whip you for a bit, pal!

Japanese: たたかれてあげましょうよ！ なるほどくん。
English: Let's go let her whip us, Mr. Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、やめておきます。
English: Now I'm DEFINITELY not going.

Japanese: えーと、病院の名前はたしか ‥‥“堀田クリニック”ッス。
English: Um... let's see... The name of the hospital... Oh yeah! The "Hotti Clinic"!

Japanese: （聞いたこと、あるような‥‥）
English: (That name sends a chill down my spine...)

Japanese: （まあ‥‥、 あとでカオを出してみるか‥‥）
English: (Well, I guess it can't hurt to stop by and say hi...)

Japanese: ほら、弁護士バッジですよ。
English: Look, it's my attorney's badge.

Japanese: おッ！　ちょっと くもってるッスね。
English: Oh! It's a little tarnished, don't you think, pal?

Japanese: 自分が、ピカピカに みがいてあげるッス！
English: Don't worry! I'll make it all sparkly shiny again!

Japanese: （なんか、 ナミダぐましいなあ‥‥）
English: (I... I'm really touched...)

Japanese: ユーカイ犯から、連絡は‥‥？
English: Have you heard anything from the kidnapper...?

Japanese: ‥‥まだ、何も。
English: ...No... not yet.

Japanese: 今日の法廷の結果を知ったら、 きっと何か言ってくるッス。
English: I'm sure once he hears what happened at the trial today, he'll call, pal.

Japanese: ‥‥くれぐれも、シゲキしちゃ ダメッスよ！
English: But remember! You can't provoke him when you get to talk to him!

Japanese: 自分はそれを見たとき、
English: When I saw that button,

Japanese: “これで王都楼の有罪は決定だ！” と思ったッスが‥‥
English: I thought to myself, "This is gonna prove Mr. Engarde's guilty!"...

Japanese: いやー、捜査はオクが深いッス。
English: But it looks like there was a lot more to it than I thought, huh pal?

Japanese: プロがそんなこと言っちゃ ダメでしょう。
English: You're a pro. You should know better than to say something like that.

Japanese: ま。ま。自分はもう、 事務員ッスから。
English: W-Well, I'm not a cop anymore. Just a regular helper around the office now, so...

Japanese: （刑事にミレンはないのか このヒト‥‥？）
English: (I wonder if he misses working down at the precinct...?)

Japanese: まさかこれで、王都楼が ステーキを食べていたとは‥‥
English: Who would've thought that this knife Mr. Engarde used to eat his dinner steak with...

Japanese: ‥‥ごくフツーの ハナシだと思いますけど。
English: Go on...

Japanese: これを死体に刺したの‥‥ 華宮　霧緒だったッス。
English: ...That it would be stabbed into the victim's body by Ms. Andrews, of all people.

Japanese: そこまで被害者のコト、 キライだったッスかねえ。
English: I guess she just hated the victim that much, huh pal?

Japanese: それより、問題は‥‥
English: The question isn't why she hated the victim to that extent, but rather

Japanese: そこまでして、王都楼さんに 罪を着せようとしたことです。
English: why she tried to pin the murder on Mr. Engarde.

Japanese: おキライだったのでしょうか？ オートロさんのこと‥‥
English: I wonder if she really hates Mr. Engarde?

Japanese: このトノサマンも 華宮　霧緒だったッスか‥‥
English: So this "Nickel Samurai" was really Ms. Andrews, huh?

Japanese: ええ。ナイフを取りに もどったところか‥‥
English: Yeah. This is either when she went to Mr. Engarde's room to get the knife...

Japanese: すべてのシゴトを終えたところか ‥‥どっちかでしょうね。
English: ...or when she was done with the foul deed. But I guess we'll never know.

Japanese: あ、そうだ。 このカード、知ってますか？
English: Oh yeah! Do you know about this card?
Card?

Japanese: 御剣のヤツは、これを見て カオ色を変えてましたけど。
English: Edgeworth, for some reason, went pale the instant he saw this card.

Japanese: そりゃァ、知ってるッス。
English: Hey, I know what this is, pal!

Japanese: え！
English: You do?

Japanese: こりゃアンタ、どう見ても サザエッス。
English: No matter what way you look at it, I'd say it's a picture of a shell.

Japanese: あの。それだけですか？
English: Um, that's it?

Japanese: あ、そうそう！
English: Oh yeah! That's right!

Japanese: ちなみに御剣検事は、サザエの ツボ焼きが大好きッス。
English: Mr. Edgeworth really likes those cooked snail things.

Japanese: あ、イヤ。あれは、なんとかいう カタツムリだったッスっけ‥‥？
English: Um, what are they called again...? "Escargot" or something like that...?

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Mr. Nick!

Japanese: 検事さんのカオ色が変わったナゾ、 解けましたね！
English: I think we just solved the mystery of why Mr. Edgeworth's face turned pale, right!?

Japanese: （‥‥やっぱり、知らされてない のか‥‥この刑事さんには）
English: (As I suspected, Gumshoe has no clue...)

Japanese: これなんですけど‥‥
English: Could you please take a look at this...?

Japanese: あー‥‥
English: Um...

Japanese: 特に、何も 思い浮かばないッス。
English: I can't think of anything to say about this, pal.

Japanese: お、おわびに ソーメン、ゴチソウするッス。
English: Wh-Why don't I make us some instant noodles, instead?

Japanese: い、いいですよベツに。
English: Th-That's OK... Really.

Japanese: ‥‥ヒトもうらやむ “刑事ヅラ”だったッス。
English: People are jealous of my roguishly cool "detective look", pal.

Japanese: このカオを買われて、刑事の テストにも合格したッス。
English: It's because of this look that I passed the detective's test, you know.

Japanese: え。
English: Oh.

Japanese: でも、やっぱり‥‥カオだけじゃあ ダメッスね、ニンゲンは。
English: But you know, people can't coast through life on their looks alone, pal.

Japanese: ダメだって。カオだけじゃ。
English: Yeah, you can't count on your looks alone.

Japanese: はああ‥‥ベンキョウに なりますねえ‥‥。
English: Wow... I learned something new today...

Japanese: ゲンキ出すッスよ。 負けちゃダメッス。
English: Cheer up! You can't give in, missy!

Japanese: は‥‥はいッ！
English: Y-Yeah!

Japanese: あ、そうだ！
English: Hey! I know!

Japanese: 今、イイもの見せてあげるッス。 ホンモノのピストルを‥‥。
English: I'll show you something cool! How's this? It's a real, genuine pistol...

Japanese: ピストルも警察手帳も ‥‥もう持ってなかったッス。
English: I... I don't have my pistol or my police badge anymore...

Japanese: ゲンキ出してください！ 負けてはダメです！
English: Cheer up! You can't give in!

Japanese: は‥‥はいッ！
English: Y-Yeah!

Japanese: （ナニやってんだよ‥‥）
English: (What are those two doing...?)

Japanese: あんな御剣検事は はじめてッス。
English: That's the first time I've seen that side of Mr. Edgeworth, pal.

Japanese: 思いどおりに証言させるため、 ナリフリかまわない‥‥
English: Forcing people to say what he wants them to during testimony...

Japanese: いったい、ナニが あったッスかねえ‥‥
English: I want to know what in the world happened to him all this time he's been gone...

Japanese: だいじょうぶッス！ きっと、また会えるッス！
English: It'll be OK! You'll see her again, little missy!

Japanese: そ‥‥そうですよね！
English: Y-Yeah!

Japanese: あきらめちゃダメッスよ！ それがカンジンッス！
English: It's really important that you don't give up!

Japanese: は‥‥はい！
English: O-OK!

Japanese: （やっぱり、声がデカいと 説得力があるな‥‥）
English: (I guess a big voice really does give you a sense of presence...)

Japanese: わたくし‥‥ あきらめませんとも！
English: I... I won't give up. Ever!

Japanese: 狙撃‥‥されたんですよね。 だいじょうぶなんですか？
English: Is she alright...? After being shot, I mean...

Japanese: 彼女のキズは、浅いッス。 ‥‥撃たれたワリには。
English: Her wound isn't that bad... Well, the gunshot wound anyway...

Japanese: でも‥‥ココロに受けた キズは、そうとう深いッス。
English: But the wound to her pride, now that's a different story, pal.

Japanese: ココロに‥‥？
English: The wound to her pride?

Japanese: まあ、今まで守ってきた “狩魔”のカンバンが、
English: Well, until now, she's always upheld the "Von Karma Creed",

Japanese: ハデにぶっ倒されたわけッス からねえ‥‥アンタに。
English: but since she came here, well, you've given her pride quite a beating, pal.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: Oh...

Japanese: なんと言っても、彼女はまだ １８才の女の子ッス。
English: I mean, she may act all grown- up, but she's really still just an 18 year old.

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっぴり、シンパイッス。
English: ...Quite frankly, I worry about her, pal.

Japanese: 自分はあまり 活劇にはくわしくないッスが‥‥
English: I'm not a real fan of action shows or anything...

Japanese: 藤見野　イサオといえば、 さすがに知ってるッス。
English: But I know who Juan Corrida and Matt Engarde are.

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟の人気をうばおうと 必死だったッス。
English: Juan Corrida, though, he was always desperately trying to be better than Matt Engarde.

Japanese: この男のために、真宵さんを ユーカイするなんて‥‥
English: I can't believe it. Maya getting kidnapped all because of this guy...

Japanese: いったい、ハンニンは どんなヤツッスかねえ！
English: I mean, what kind of person is the real murderer anyway!?

Japanese: やっぱり、オートロさんの 大ファンのかた、でしょうか？
English: Maybe they're a big fan of Mr. Engarde?

Japanese: （‥‥あの、誘拐犯の声‥‥）
English: (But the kidnapper's voice...)

Japanese: （なんていうか、 おじいさん‥‥に聞こえたけど）
English: (He sounded like an old man to me...)

Japanese: 彼女のイメージが、 だいぶ変わったッス。
English: My impression of her has totally changed, pal.

Japanese: あの‥‥彼女は、今‥‥？
English: Um... So, where is she right now...?

Japanese: 警察で事情聴取されてる はずッスよ。
English: I'm sure she's being questioned down at the precinct.

Japanese: おそらく、死体の破損や 捜査をカクランした容疑で、
English: At the very least about messing with the body, and obstructing the investigation.

Japanese: 留置所に入れられることに なるのでは‥‥
English: And she'll probably be staying over at the detention center...

Japanese: （留置所、か‥‥）
English: (The detention center, huh...)

Japanese: 彼女は、華宮　霧緒の 先輩だったッス。
English: She was Ms. Adrian Andrews' mentor.

Japanese: （たよりきっていた先輩が 自殺してしまって‥‥）
English: (Because of the suicide of the mentor she depended on,)

Japanese: （華宮　霧緒は、あとを追って 自分も死のうとした‥‥）
English: (Adrian Andrews tried to follow her in an attempted suicide...)

Japanese: （じゃあ、今の彼女は だれに“依存”を‥‥？）
English: (Now the question is, who is Ms. Andrews relying on now...?)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 成歩堂法律事務所
English: March 22 Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: おッ！　お帰りッス。
English: Hey! Welcome back, pal!

Japanese: カンタンな夜食、 用意しといたッス。
English: I thought I'd make you a little something for dinner.

Japanese: そ‥‥それはどうも。
English: Th...That's nice... Thanks.

Japanese: ゴウセイに、フルコースッス。 ‥‥カンヅメの。
English: It's a rich-man's luxurious full course meal... out of a can, that is...

Japanese: せっかくですけど‥‥ 食べてる時間、ありませんから。
English: I'm sorry you went through all the trouble to cook, but I don't have the time to eat.

Japanese: あ。カンキリがないッス。
English: Oh, hey... You don't have a can opener here, pal.

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ‥‥）
English: (You've got to be kidding... And here I thought he had already whipped something up.)

Japanese: ‥‥どッスか！ 自分の出番はないッスか！
English: Oh, I know! There is one way I know how to be helpful!

Japanese: なんでも相談に乗るッスよ！
English: Ask me about anything you want, pal! Go ahead!

Japanese: （せっかくだから‥‥ 何か相談してみるか？）
English: (Well, since he's here and offering... I wonder what I should try asking him about?)

Japanese: あの。“電波障害”って‥‥？
English: Um... So what is "electromagnetic interference"...?

Japanese: 受信装置に他の電波が干渉して、 雑音が発生することがあるッス。
English: It's something that happens when a radio wave gets mixed up with another signal, pal.

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。そういえば。
English: Oh, when you put it that way...

Japanese: わたくし、わかりません‥‥。
English: I don't understand what you're talking about...

Japanese: たとえば、パソコンのそばで 携帯電話が鳴ると、
English: Like for example, when a cell phone goes off next to a computer screen,

Japanese: ディスプレイの表示が ゆがんだりするッス。
English: the stuff on the screen gets kinda fuzzy and starts acting funny, right?

Japanese: はい？ “ぱそこん”‥‥ですか？
English: Huh? ..."Kom-pu-ter"?

Japanese: ほら。テレビのそばでドライヤーを 使うと、ザザッてなるでしょ？
English: Um, it's like when you use the dryer next to the TV, and the screen starts looking weird.

Japanese: あ。ああ、はい！　なります！ ‥‥ええ、それはもう！
English: O-Oh, yes, the TV does do that! Hmm... Oh, so that's what you're talking about!

Japanese: （リカイできて、 異様にうれしそうだな‥‥）
English: (She seems amazingly happy at being able to understand this...)

Japanese: アンタのトランシーバーに 雑音が入った部屋‥‥
English: So the room you were in when that interference to the transceiver happened...

Japanese: ‥‥そこに何か、強い電波を出す モノがあったのかもしれないッス！
English: There's gotta be something there that's sending out very strong radio waves, pal!

Japanese: たとえば、そッスね‥‥。 ‥‥盗聴器、とか。
English: Something like... Hmm... Like a listening device or something.
Ah!

Japanese: アンタ！　その雑音は、どこで？
English: Hey, speaking of that! Where were you when it happened?

Japanese: イサオさんの控え室‥‥ 殺人現場です！
English: We were in Mr. Corrida's room... The scene of the murder!

Japanese: なんスとぉぉッ！
English: What!?

Japanese: 自分は警察に忍びこんで、 電波探知機を持ち出してくるッス！
English: That's it! I'm going to sneak into the precinct and get a bug sweeper!

Japanese: じゃ、あとで！ 待ち合わせ場所は、殺人現場ッス！
English: I'll meet you at the crime scene later, alright pal!?

Japanese: あ。ちょっと！　イトノコさん！
English: Ah, wait! Gumshoe!

Japanese: うおおおおお！　捜査ッス！
English: Oh yeah, baby! It's investigating time!

Japanese: 血が鳴るッス！ ウデがさわぐッス！
English: I'm on fire, pal! My fingers are itching to go!

Japanese: ソーサッスゥゥゥゥゥゥゥゥゥゥ！
English: Yeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeah!

Japanese: なるほどくん！ わたくしたちも、まいりましょう！
English: We should be going too, Mr. Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥よし、行こう！
English: Alright, let's go!

Japanese: ‥‥あ。わたくし、 お水をあげますね！
English: Oh! I'll water it!

Japanese: い、いや。もう ぼくがあげたから‥‥
English: Ah, it's OK... I already did that...

Japanese: ‥‥じょうろ、じょうろ‥‥
English: ...The watering can... Where are you, Mr. Watering Can...?

Japanese: （春美ちゃん、何かしてないと 不安なんだ‥‥）
English: (Pearls gets too wound up if she's not doing something to distract herself...)

Japanese: （チャーリーくんには、 ちょっとガマンしてもらうか）
English: (I hope Charley can withstand Typhoon Pearls...)

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本が あざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: あ。何かホーリツをお調べに なるのですか！
English: Oh! Do you need to look something up in one of these law books, Mr. Nick!?

Japanese: ど、どの本が！ どの本がヒツヨウなのですかッ！
English: Wh-Which book is it!? Which book do you need!?

Japanese: ううう‥‥漢字が読めないし、 背もとどきません‥‥
English: Umm... I can't read those hard books, and... um... I can't reach them either...

Japanese: い、いいよ。 気をつかわなくて。
English: I-It's OK, Pearls. Really.

Japanese: ぼくのデスクだ。
English: My desk.

Japanese: あまり座る機会がないため、 いやにキチンとかたづいている。
English: Since I don't have time to sit, it's unusually clean...

Japanese: あ。わたくしがゆうべ、 さらにキチンとかたづけました！
English: Oh! I cleaned it up even more for you last night!

Japanese: トランシーバー‥‥ッスか？
English: The transceiver...?

Japanese: あっ、なるほどくん。 あのことを話してみては？
English: Oh, Mr. Nick! You should ask Mr. Scruffy Detective about that thing.

Japanese: あのこと？
English: What thing?

Japanese: そうだ！　コイツ‥‥急に コワれちゃったんですよ！
English: Oh yeah! This thing just up and broke all of a sudden!

Japanese: コショウ‥‥ッスか？
English: It... It broke, pal?

Japanese: 誘拐犯と話していたら、 急に雑音が入って‥‥
English: When I was talking to the kidnapper, it just suddenly broke into static...

Japanese: ホラ。こんなふうに。
English: Look, it sounded like this.

Japanese: 雑音なんか、聞こえねッス。
English: I don't hear any static, pal.

Japanese: あ、あれれ？
English: H-Huh??

Japanese: ‥‥なおってしまいましたね。
English: Maybe it fixed itself?

Japanese: おかしいな‥‥。 あんなにザーザー言ってたのに。
English: That's strange... I'm sure it was making a loud static noise.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥もしかすると‥‥
English: Hmm... Maybe...

Japanese: なんですか？
English: Maybe what?

Japanese: “電波障害” ‥‥かもしれないッス。
English: Maybe it was "electromagnetic interference", pal.

Japanese: でんぱしょうがい？
English: "Electromagnetic interference"?

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: March 22 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: （看守の話によれば‥‥）
English: (From what the guard told me...)

Japanese: （オートロさんといっしょに、 霧緒さんも留置されてるらしい）
English: (It sounds like along with Mr. Engarde, Ms. Andrews is also being detained here.)

Japanese: じゃあサッソク、お話を‥‥
English: Then we should talk with them since we're here...

Japanese: ふたりとも 取り調べ中だってさ。
English: Yeah, but both of them are still in questioning.

Japanese: ううう‥‥時間がないのに。
English: Hmm... And we don't have time to waste.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥もう面会時間、 終わっちまうぞ‥‥）
English: (Yeah, visiting hours are almost over...)

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitor's the visitor's room.

Japanese: 今はヒマなのだろう。 立ったまま寝ている。
English: Catching a few z's while he's standing; now that's a good way to spend free time.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile, you're on candid camera.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: March 22 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: すみません。今日はもう、 面会時間は終了しました。
English: I'm sorry, but visiting hours are over for today.
Aw...

Japanese: （そんな‥‥聞きたいことが いっぱいあるのに‥‥）
English: (Argh... I have too many questions I need to ask...)

Japanese: す、すみません。ぼく、 弁護士の成歩堂といいますけど‥‥
English: I-I'm sorry, but I'm Phoenix Wright, a lawyer for one of the...

Japanese: ナルホドーさん‥‥？
English: You're Mr. Wright, you say?

Japanese: あ。そういえば、あなたに メッセージがありますよ。
English: Oh, yeah, there's a message here for you.

Japanese: メッセージ‥‥
English: A message...?

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟からです。 ‥‥ハイ、どうぞ。
English: It's from Matt Engarde. ...Ah, here you are.

Japanese: な、何が書いてあるのですか！ ダイジな話でしょうか‥‥？
English: What did he write!? Is it something really important...?

Japanese: わからない。 ‥‥とりあえず、読んでみよう。
English: I don't know. Well, let's see what it has to say...

Japanese: “弁護士さんへ。 ダイジな話があります”
English: "To the lawyer dude. I've got something really important to tell you."

Japanese: （なんだろう‥‥ ムナさわぎがする‥‥）
English: (Why do I feel uneasy all of a sudden...?)

Japanese: あ、成歩堂さん。
English: Oh, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥じつは‥‥ おねがいがあるんですよ。
English: So actually... I have a favor to ask of you.

Japanese: ボクの飼ってるネコ‥‥ シュウっていうんだけど。
English: I have this cat named Shoe.

Japanese: エサ、少ししか用意してないから、 おなかをすかせてると思うんです。
English: I didn't put out a lot of food when I left the house, so he's probably pretty hungry.

Japanese: ちょっと、ボクの家まで行って、 エサ、やっといてくれませんか？
English: You think you could drop by my house and feed Shoe for me, dude?

Japanese: ボクの家、ホテルの先に ありますから。
English: My house is just a little ways down from the hotel, alright?

Japanese: た‥‥タイヘンです！ すぐ行ってあげましょう！
English: Th-This is terrible! Let's hurry! We have to feed his cat!

Japanese: シュウちゃん、きっと おなかペコペコですよ！
English: I'm sure poor Shoe's stomach is growling by now!

Japanese: そ‥‥そうだね。
English: Y-Yeah, I guess...

Japanese: 《王都楼のメモ》を ポケットにネジこんだ。
English: Matt's Note jammed into a pocket.

Japanese: （依頼人のたのみだ。 ‥‥行っておくか）
English: (A client's request is a request... Guess I should go check up on his cat.)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 ホテル・バンドー　ロビー
English: March 22 Gatewater Hotel Hotel Lobby

Japanese: さあ、なるほどくん！
English: Alright now, Mr. Nick!

Japanese: 手がかりを探しましょう！ 真宵さまのために‥‥
English: Let's go look for clues! We have to, for Mystic Maya's sake...

Japanese: そうは行かないョッ！
English: You shall not pass!

Japanese: オバチャンッ！
English: *gasp* Ms. Oldbag!

Japanese: 気やすく呼ぶんじゃないよ！ このギザギザ男がッ！
English: Don't devalue my name and turn it into a gasp, you spiky-headed poof!

Japanese: アンタのせいで、オバチャンまた、 ワルモノにされちまったョ！
English: Because of you, I've been made to look like the bad guy again! Although, I did get a

Japanese: ヤクソクどおり、ミッちゃんにガム もらったけどサ‥‥オバチャンが
English: piece of gum from Edgey-boy, just as he promised... But what I really wanted was

Japanese: ほしかったのは、そんなモンじゃ ないんだョ！　アイでショアイ！
English: something much more valuable! I wanted Edgey-poo's heart! I want it all for me!

Japanese: もお、オバチャンの野生が目ざめ ちゃったからねェアンタのせいで
English: It's all your fault! You've awakened the wild beast inside of this Oldbag!

Japanese: うぐぐぐぐぐぐぐぐぐぐぐぐぐぐぐ
English: Grrrrrrrrrrrrrrrrrrrrrrrrr!

Japanese: きゃあ！　おばさまッ！
English: Aah! Ms. Oldbag!

Japanese: さわるんじゃないョ！
English: Keep your hands off of me!

Japanese: ッたく、このヘルメット！ 空気も入ってこないんだからネ！
English: This helmet is airtight! No air's gets in and no air gets out!

Japanese: あの‥‥どうして ヘルメットを‥‥？
English: Umm... What does your helmet have to do with anything?

Japanese: フン！　オバチャン、カラに 閉じこもっちゃったんだからネ！
English: Hmph! Don't think you can get me to move with silly questions!

Japanese: この先に進みたかったら、 オバチャンを倒してからにしな！
English: You're going to have to defeat me if you want to get by!

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥）
English: (I'm not hearing this...)

Japanese: （そうだ‥‥御剣からもらった こいつを‥‥）
English: (Hmm... Maybe if I show her this letter I got from Edgeworth...)

Japanese: あの、オバチャン、これを‥‥
English: Um... Ms. Oldbag... If you would look at...

Japanese: なんだい！　このこきたない
English: What!? You want me to look at this worthless piece of...

Japanese: ‥‥んミッちゃあん‥‥
English: ...Edgey-pooooo...

Japanese: （うっ‥‥ノーミツな フェロモンが‥‥）
English: (Ugh... Is that her perfume, Phéromone d'Amour, I smell...? *shudder*)

Japanese: ‥‥なになに。
English: Let's see here...

Japanese: “この青くトガった青年に、 ベンギをはかってはくれまいか”
English: "Would you please allow this unsophisticated young person to conduct his investigation?"

Japanese: “あなたの御剣　怜侍より”
English: "Yours truly, Miles Edgeworth"

Japanese: あ、“あなたの”ですか！
English: "Y-Yours truly"!?

Japanese: フン！　文学的コチョー ってヤツだよ。
English: Hmph, that man's good at flattery.

Japanese: ま。ミッちゃんのたのみじゃ しょうがないねェ。
English: Fine. But only because Edgey-poo said so, you understand!?

Japanese: 《御剣の紹介状》を オバチャンにあげた。
English: Letter of Introduction given to Ms. Oldbag.

Japanese: オバチャン、ちょっと 用を思い出しちゃったョ。
English: I just thought of something I have to do.

Japanese: いいかい！　ワルさ するんじゃないョ！
English: Remember, no messing around! You do anything bad and I won't let you off the hook!

Japanese: おばさま‥‥ミツルギ検事に ムチュウなのですねえ‥‥
English: It looks like she has strong feelings for Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: まあ、なんのイミもないけどね。
English: That may be, but you know nothing's going to come of it.

Japanese: ナニを言うのですかっ！ 気持ちはかならず伝わるモノです！
English: That's so mean, Mr. Nick! Feelings are meant to be told and shared!

Japanese: いてて‥‥
English: Owww...

Japanese: （こういうハナシになると ムキになるんだよなあ‥‥）
English: (Every time we talk about love, I always end up with a handprint on my face somehow.)

Japanese: とにかく、調査を始めようか。
English: Um, so anyway, let's continue our investigation.

Japanese: はいっ！
English: OK!

Japanese: カタカタカタカタ‥‥
English: *rata-tat-tat-tat-tat...*

Japanese: わっ！　な、なんですか！
English: Ack! What!? What now!?

Japanese: ひとつ、言い忘れてたケド。
English: One little thing, before I forget.

Japanese: 王都楼の控え室はネ。 今日は入っちゃダメだョ。
English: You can't go into Engarde's room today.

Japanese: どうしてですか？
English: Why?

Japanese: なんでもネ。捜査本部として 使うんだそうな。１日だけ。
English: The police's main investigation team is going to be in there all day, you hear?

Japanese: （虎狼死家の捜査‥‥か）
English: (I wonder if they're the team in charge of investigating "De Killer"...?)

Japanese: 入るんじゃないよ！ オバチャンが怒られるから。
English: So don't go in there! Set one foot in there and you'll face the wrath of Wendy Oldbag!

Japanese: コラッ！　キョロキョロ するんじゃないョ！
English: Hey! I said no restless loitering and no touching!

Japanese: オバチャン、トコトン ジャマしちゃうからねェ！
English: You're interfering with my watch, and that's a no-no!

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ。 調査を進めるためには‥‥）
English: (Argh. Looks like in order to search around here...)

Japanese: （まず、この宇宙人を なんとかしないと‥‥）
English: (I have to first get this kooky "alien" to go away...)

Japanese: 取材に来た人たちの カメラだろう。
English: These cameras must belong to the press that came to cover the conference.

Japanese: どれも本格的なシロモノで、 ステージをねらっている。
English: They're all basically doing the same thing: staring at the empty stage.

Japanese: 吹き抜けのロビーは、 とにかく大きな空間に感じる。
English: A lobby with a grand staircase always feels enormous.

Japanese: そして今、この空間には、 事件の緊張感がみなぎっている。
English: But right now, the only thing filling this enormous room is anxiety about the murder.

Japanese: ‥‥これ以上、悪いことなんて 起きようがないんだけど‥‥。
English: ...I really hope nothing else bad happens here...

Japanese: アトラクション・ショーの ステージだ。
English: It's the stage they were going to use for the post-ceremony stage show.

Japanese: ショーのあと、記者会見も 予定されていたというけど‥‥
English: After the show, there was supposed to be a press conference, but...

Japanese: でも、その記者会見‥‥
English: But that press conference...

Japanese: 被害者の藤見野さんが、オートロ さんを告発するつもりだったんだ。
English: It was really the victim, Juan Corrida, trying to disclose something about Mr. Engarde.

Japanese: 同じヒーローなのに‥‥ どうして、そんなことを。
English: But they're both heroes... Why would they do something so un-heroic?

Japanese: 見た目はヒーローだけど‥‥ 中身はニンゲンだからね。
English: They may look like heroes on the outside, but on the inside, they're only human.

Japanese: アトラクション・ショーと 記者会見のために用意されたイス。
English: Seats for the spectators of the post-ceremony show and press conference.

Japanese: ‥‥両方とも、 ムダになってしまった。
English: But it's too bad neither event is going to be held now.

Japanese: あの‥‥
English: Um...

Japanese: カタカタカタカタ‥‥
English: *rata-tat-tat-tat-tat...*

Japanese: ハン！　このオバチャンに モノを尋ねようなんざ‥‥、
English: Hah!

Japanese: １００万光年早いんだョッ！
English: You're a million light-years too early to be asking ME questions, whippersnapper!

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ。 調査を進めるためには‥‥）
English: (Argh. Looks like the only way I'm going to get any investigating done...)

Japanese: （まず、この宇宙人を なんとかしないと‥‥）
English: (is to first do something about this kooky "alien"...)

Japanese: あの。 これなんですけど‥‥
English: Umm... Could you please take a look at this...?

Japanese: フン！　ヘルメットがくもって よく見えないネ！
English: Hmph! Sorry, sonny. I can't see it very well through this helmet!

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ。 調査を進めるためには‥‥）
English: (Argh. Looks like the only way I'm going to get any investigating done...)

Japanese: （まず、この宇宙人を なんとかしないと‥‥）
English: (is to first do something about this kooky "alien"...)

Japanese: うちゅーじんうちゅーじん 言うんじゃないョッ！
English: I see that look in your eyes! You stop thinking I'm an alien from outer space this instant!

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 ホテル・バンドー　すみれの間
English: March 22 Gatewater Hotel Viola Hall

Japanese: だれもいないね。
English: Looks like we're the only ones here.

Japanese: でも‥‥なんだか、 あわただしいですね‥‥
English: And yet... the hotel seems so busy somehow...

Japanese: （“殺し屋”の手がかりを 追っているのかもしれないな）
English: (Probably because the police team is scouring for clues about "De Killer"...)

Japanese: また、真宵さまと お食事をしたいです‥‥
English: I want to eat a meal with Mystic Maya again...

Japanese: ‥‥そうだね。
English: Yeah, me too...

Japanese: 真宵さまの食べっぷりを 見てると‥‥、
English: Whenever I watch Mystic Maya eat like she does...

Japanese: わたくしも“食べるぞ” という気分になります。
English: It makes me happy about eating, and then I can eat a lot.

Japanese: よし。
English: Well, then...

Japanese: この事件が解決したら、みんなに フルコースをごちそうするよ。
English: How about after we wrap up this case, we all go out for a huge twenty-course feast!

Japanese: はい！ がんばりましょう！
English: OK! Let's work really hard, then!

Japanese: 料理はまだ かたづけられていない。
English: They haven't cleaned up all the food yet.

Japanese: パーティーは、終わってしまうと とてもサビシイ気がするんだ。
English: There's a sad feeling hanging in the air now that the party is over...

Japanese: 授賞式が行われたステージだ。
English: The awards ceremony was held on that stage.

Japanese: ‥‥ゴージャスだったな、 さすがに。
English: It was really fabulous.

Japanese: ちょっと、あのサーカスを 思い出しました。
English: You just reminded me of the circus for a second.

Japanese: みなさん、おゲンキでしょうか？
English: I wonder if everyone is alright.

Japanese: （タチミ・サーカスは最近、 公演を再開したらしい）
English: (I heard that Berry Big Circus just recently started holding performances again...)

Japanese: ‥‥きっと、ゲンキに やってると思うよ。
English: I'm sure they're all fine, Pearls.

Japanese: それにしても、 ゴウカなホテルだ。
English: This sure is one luxurious hotel.

Japanese: 何かにとりつかれたかのように、 ゴテゴテかざりつけされている。
English: Almost to the point of gaudy, with how it blends together everything "fancy" imaginable.

Japanese: （そういえば去年、あのボーイさん から暑中見舞いがきてたっけ）
English: (Speaking of fancy, didn't that bellboy give me something like that last year?)

Japanese: 大げさなロビーに通じる、 大げさなドアが見える。
English: There's a grand set of doors over there.

Japanese: ボーイにつれられた真宵ちゃんが 消えたドアだ。
English: It's the doors Maya followed the bellboy out of, only to disappear...

Japanese: ‥‥あのとき、ぼくたちが ついていれば‥‥
English: ...If only we had all gone together...

Japanese: わたくし、このような キレイなもの、初めて見ました。
English: That is such a beautiful sight.

Japanese: シャンデリア？
English: The chandelier?

Japanese: はい。‥‥こんな美しい 明かりの下で、
English: Yes. But I can't believe it...

Japanese: このようなヒドい事件が 起こるなんて‥‥
English: I can't believe that such a terrible murder happened under such beautiful lights...

Japanese: ショックです。
English: It's shocking.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 控え室前　ろうか
English: March 22 Gatewater Hotel Hallway

Japanese: お、ナルホドー。
English: Hey, City Boy!

Japanese: ナツミさん‥‥ まだ、いたんですか？
English: L-Lotta... You're still here...

Japanese: メチャロンやッ！
English: Reckon course!

Japanese: カメラマンは、スクープで おまんま食べてるんやで！
English: An investigative photographer eats or starves on her ability to snap up the scoop, yeah!?

Japanese: なんや、このホテル、 ミョーなフンイキ、しとるからナ。
English: And this hotel just has that "aura of mystery".

Japanese: なんか起こらんかいなー、って。
English: Ya know, like something's always about to happen.

Japanese: でも‥‥カメラは 持っていらっしゃるのですか？
English: But... Do you have a camera?

Japanese: メチャリマエやッ！
English: Reckgiven!

Japanese: カメラマンにとって、カメラは おまんまを盛るおちゃわんやッ！
English: A photographer's gotta have cameras out the ear like corn to be a real pro, ya know!?

Japanese: ちゃんと、ここにこうして
English: So I'm hangin' around here...

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、ウチの おちゃわん、盗まれたんやったワ。
English: Speaking of cameras and feedin' the mouth, do ya have mine, ya bread-thief!?

Japanese: （それを忘れるか‥‥？）
English: (Why can't you drop that "thief" thing already?)

Japanese: じゃあウチ、 そろそろ帰るワ。
English: Well, I reckon it's time for me to get going.

Japanese: カメラのないカメラマンなんて、 ただの“マン”や。
English: A tabloid photographer without a camera is just a "tabloid", huh.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Umm... Yeah, I guess so.

Japanese: ほな。明日のサイバン、 見に行くからナ。
English: Keep yerself together out there, ya hear!? I'm comin' to see ya in court tomorrow.

Japanese: ‥‥ありがとうございます。
English: O-OK, I'll see you then...

Japanese: おじょうちゃんも、 がんばるんやで。
English: And you too there, little 'un. Keep up the good work, OK?

Japanese: はい。
English: OK!

Japanese: スキキライしちゃアカンで。
English: Don't be picky about yer food, now.

Japanese: はい。
English: OK!

Japanese: シュクダイは、 ちゃんとやるんやで。
English: And make sure ya do all yer homework, ya hear?

Japanese: はい。
English: OK!

Japanese: それから、もしカメラ拾ったら、 すぐネーチャンのところに‥‥
English: And if you happen to find yerself a camera, make sure ya bring it right to me, ya--

Japanese: 早く帰ってください！
English: Would you please just leave already!?

Japanese: まあ、愛らしい。
English: Aww, how lovely!

Japanese: クマさん、お花にかこまれて とてもうれしそうですね。
English: The teddy bear looks happy to be surrounded by so many beautiful flowers.

Japanese: （春美ちゃん、やっぱり 好きみたいだな、ぬいぐるみ）
English: (Pearls really seems to like teddy bears a lot.)

Japanese: あ。もしかして、ナツミさんも‥‥
English: Ah... I wonder if... Lotta also...

Japanese: とってへん！　とってへんでえ！ ウチやない！　ゼッタイやッ！
English: I didn't touch them! Wasn't me, I tell ya! I give ya my word!

Japanese: （どうやらナツミさんが １ケ、シッケイしたらしい）
English: (Why do I get the impression Lotta helped herself to one of them...?)

Japanese: “王都楼さん江 英都プロダクション一同”‥‥
English: "To Mr. Engarde From the Global Studio Staff"

Japanese: 弁護士にも花輪が あればいいのにな。
English: Ah, it'd be nice if lawyers got flowers too. Something like,

Japanese: “成歩堂さん江 依頼人一同”‥‥とか。
English: "To Mr. Wright From All Your Grateful Clients".

Japanese: ドアに張り紙がある。 “王都楼　真悟様　控え室”
English: There's a piece of paper taped to the door that says, "Matt Engarde's Room".

Japanese: おばさま、おっしゃってましたね。 “今夜は、入ってはいけない”
English: The old lady told us about this, didn't she? That we can't go in this room tonight.

Japanese: （中では、捜査会議が開かれて いるんだろうな）
English: (I wonder if an investigation briefing is going on right now inside...)

Japanese: （イトノコ刑事、ヌキで‥‥）
English: (Without Detective Gumshoe...)

Japanese: トイレなら、それぞれの 部屋にあるのだが、
English: There are toilets in each room in this hotel,

Japanese: このホテルでは、いろいろな イベントが開かれるので、
English: but since all sorts of events are held here,

Japanese: 泊まっている客以外の人たちが 使うのだろう。
English: there are bathrooms for people who aren't staying here to use as well.

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくもそのひとりだけど。
English: Perfect for people like me, who can't afford a stay here in the first place...

Japanese: いろんな花が、ゴージャスに 咲きみだれている。
English: Compared to the flowers on the other side of the hall, these are much more gorgeous.

Japanese: ‥‥ひまわりとチューリップ以外、 花の名前は知らないけど。
English: Although, other than sunflowers and tulips, I have no idea what any of them are.

Japanese: あ。なるほどくん。 この花はなんですか？
English: Um, Mr. Nick? What's this flower called?

Japanese: ひまわり、かな。
English: It's a sunflower... I think.

Japanese: こちらの花も、きれいですね。
English: This flower over here is pretty too.

Japanese: それはチューリップ、だね。
English: That's a tulip, I guess.

Japanese: ナルホドー。そのラインナップじゃ さすがに、コドモもだませへんで。
English: Callin' everythin' a sunflower or a tulip; don't reckon even little kids'll fall for that!

Japanese: “藤見野　イサオ様　控え室” 張り紙にはそう書かれている。
English: There's a piece of paper taped to the door that says, "Juan Corrida's Room".

Japanese: そこがサツジン現場、 というワケや。
English: So that's the scene of the murder, I reckon.

Japanese: ナニかが起こる、とは 思っとったけど、
English: I thought somethin' had gone on that night,

Japanese: まさかサツジンとはなー。
English: but I didn't never think it was murder.

Japanese: 楽屋の前に花があるのは ふつうだけど、
English: I can understand flowers in front of the dressing rooms,

Japanese: なんでこんなところに クマのぬいぐるみが‥‥？
English: but what are stuffed bears doing here...?

Japanese: もしかして、クマが大好きな アクション・スターなのか‥‥？
English: Could it be that there's an action star who has a soft spot for teddy bears...?

Japanese: ‥‥まさか、ね。
English: ...Nah, can't be.

Japanese: 向かいの花輪にくらべて、 さらにゴージャスだ。
English: Compared to the flowers on the other side of the hall, these are much more gorgeous.

Japanese: 事務所、レコード会社、 ファンクラブ、兄弟一同‥‥
English: Let's see... Record companies, fan clubs, company workers, family...

Japanese: 持って帰るのが タイヘンそうだな。
English: Carrying all these flowers home would be hard, I think...

Japanese: “藤見野　イサオ様　控え室” 張り紙にはそう書かれている。
English: There's a piece of paper taped to the door that says, "Juan Corrida's Room".

Japanese: （このドアの向こうで、 いったい何があったのか‥‥？）
English: (I wonder what EXACTLY happened in the room behind this door...?)

Japanese: （ぼくたちには、まだ わかっていないんだ‥‥）
English: (Even now, no one really has any idea...)

Japanese: 事件当夜のことですけど。
English: I want to ask you about the night of the murder.

Japanese: なんや？ 情報は高ォつくでえ！
English: What? Ya really gonna shell out the bucks for the info I got!?

Japanese: ナツミさん、控え室の前に 張りこんでいたんですよね？
English: Lotta, you were loitering in this hallway the night of the murder, were you not?

Japanese: まあナ。‥‥ただし。
English: Well, kinda... But...

Japanese: （‥‥うわあ、出た）
English: (Brace yourself, Wright. Here it comes...)

Japanese: ずっと見張っとったワケやないで。 スターさんもウロついとったし‥‥
English: I didn't exactly hang around here the entire time, ya know. Followed a few stars around...

Japanese: サインもろたり、アクシュしたり、 お茶したり。
English: got a few autographs, shook a few hands, had a soda-pop with a few of 'em too.

Japanese: （とても“見張っていた”なんて 言えない状況だな‥‥）
English: (Looks like she wasn't here the entire time, that night...)

Japanese: 警備員のおばさまも、ずっと ここにいたわけではありません。
English: The security lady also wasn't in this hallway the whole time either.

Japanese: （控え室に出入りするのは、 だれにでもできたわけか‥‥）
English: (I guess this means there's no one who can tell us who came and went that night...)

Japanese: あの‥‥カメラケースに入っていた メモのことなんですけど。
English: So, about the note that was inside your camera case.

Japanese: ああ。ウチが書いた、アレ？
English: Oh, that ditty I wrote?

Japanese: あの情報‥‥ 少しは信用できるんですか？
English: Yeah... Can I believe what you've written?

Japanese: オートロが、自分のマネージャーを 藤見野に送りつけた、ってヤツ？
English: Ya mean the stuff about Engarde shovin' his manager lady onto Corrida?

Japanese: ええ。
English: Yeah...

Japanese: いやー、やめといたほうが エエんとちゃうかあ。
English: Ah, well, I reckon ya best not be believin' that.

Japanese: え。
English: What?

Japanese: あれはホレ、ウチが気の向くまま、 テケトーに書いただけや。
English: Look, I sorta wrote that on a whim, ya know? Writin' whatever came to mind.

Japanese: “テケトーに”‥‥？
English: "Whatever came to mind"...?

Japanese: ひらたく言えば、 デタラメ、ゆうことやな。
English: Yeah, when ya get down to it, it's just a lotta random bull dooders.

Japanese: なんや、ニイちゃん。 ４つぐらいフケこんだで！
English: Hey, what's with ya!? Why ya starin' at me like my grandpa used to!?

Japanese: はあ。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: おじょーちゃんも、４センチぐらい 背ェ伸びたんちゃうかぁ。
English: Hey, and why do you look like you suddenly got older too!? Or am I just shrinkin' here!?

Japanese: ううう‥‥
English: Umm...

Japanese: そう！　それやッ！ ウチの１６万のカメラ！
English: Ah! My baby! My $1,600 baby!

Japanese: なんや、あの赤い検事！
English: What's with that red-coated prosecutor anyhow!?

Japanese: “証拠や！”‥‥とか言うて、 返してくれへんねん。
English: The guy told me it was "evidence" and refused to give it back to me!

Japanese: まあ‥‥そうかもしれませんね。
English: Well, that's kind of how it is...

Japanese: なあなあ、アンタ、 トモダチやろ？　あの赤いのと。
English: Hey, hey. Yer that red-coat's friend, ain't ya?

Japanese: うまいコト言いくるめて、 取り返してやァ、うちのカメラァ。
English: So put in a few good words for me and git me back my camera.

Japanese: む、ムチャ言わないでくださいよ！
English: Y-You want me to do what!?

Japanese: ５時間もネバれば かならず返してくれるって。
English: Listen, nag the guy real good fer 'bout five hours and I guarantee he'll give it back.

Japanese: ‥‥自分でやってください。
English: Why don't you do your own dirty work...?

Japanese: あの。ちょっと 見ていただけますか？
English: Um, would you please take a look at this?

Japanese: いや、アカン。ダメ。 もってのホカホカおしぼりやッ。
English: Nope. Sorry. No can do. Only thing I wanna see is a steamin' hand towel.

Japanese: え。‥‥ほかほか‥‥？
English: Um, what? A steaming hand towel...?

Japanese: なんか今日は、目で モノを見る気ィしないんや。
English: Need one for my eyes. Today just ain't a good day for my eyes to be lookin' at stuff.

Japanese: （そんな日があるかよ！）
English: (How convenient! Maybe I'll have to use that one next time you show me something!)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 藤見野　イサオの控え室
English: March 22 Gatewater Hotel Corrida's Hotel Room
Ooongh... Oooonnngh... Ooongh... Oooonnngh...

Japanese: ななな、なるほどくん！
English: M-M-Mr. Nick!

Japanese: こ、この世のものとも思えない、 ブキミなうめき声だ‥‥！
English: Wh-What is that otherworldly, ghastly moaning...!?

Japanese: ‥‥うぐ。‥‥ううううぐぐぐ‥‥
English: Ooonnnn... Oooo... Nnnghff... Ghfff...

Japanese: お‥‥オバケ、きらいです。 ‥‥ああああああん。
English: I... I hate evil ghosts... Waaaaaaaaaaaaah...

Japanese: いや、妖怪かもしれないぞ！
English: I don't think it's a ghost... Maybe it's a demon!?

Japanese: ダレが妖怪だいッ！
English: E-Excuse me!? Watch who you're calling a demon, brat!

Japanese: きゃあっ！
English: Aaaah!

Japanese: 出たぁ！　‥‥宇宙人だッ！
English: Zoinks! It's the alien!!

Japanese: ダレが宇宙人だいッ！
English: Who are you calling an alien!?

Japanese: カタカタカタカタ‥‥
English: *rata-tat-tat-tat-tat...*

Japanese: なんだ‥‥オバチャン、 こんなところにいたんですか。
English: Oh, it's just you, Ms. Oldbag. What are you doing here?

Japanese: なんなんだいアンタたちは！
English: What is wrong with young'uns today!?

Japanese: せっかくこう、イサオちゃんとの オモイデにひたってたのにサ！
English: I came down here to pay my respects to my poor Juan, and you're disturbing me!

Japanese: ザザ‥‥ザザザ‥‥ザ‥‥ザザ‥‥
English: hissssssss... ssssssssss... ssssss... hisssssssssss... hisssssssssssss... ssssssss...

Japanese: （‥‥このトランシーバー‥‥）
English: (...This transceiver...)

Japanese: （この部屋に来ると、雑音が 鳴り出すな‥‥）
English: (Whenever we're in this room, it starts acting up and hissing static at us...)

Japanese: どなたか、相談できるかたは いらっしゃらないでしょうか‥‥。
English: I wonder if there is someone we can talk to about this...

Japanese: がおおおおおお！
English: *GROOOOOOOOOOWL!*

Japanese: ８時だクマ！ ボク、クマオヤジ！
English: I'm Uncle Bear, and I say it's "bearly" 8 o' clock!

Japanese: なな、なんだなんだ！
English: Wh-Wh-What is that infernal racket!?

Japanese: プレゼントの時計の時報だョ。 もう、夜の８時だからネ。
English: It's one of the presents going off. Sounds like it's already 8 PM. Way past your bedtime!

Japanese: ううう‥‥死ぬかと思った。
English: Urk... That startled me! I thought I was going to die for a second...

Japanese: 午後８時‥‥
English: Eight PM...

Japanese: ちょうどあの晩、授賞式が 終わったころですね。
English: That's the time when the award ceremony ended that night, remember?

Japanese: （あれから まるまる２日、か‥‥）
English: (Time sure flies... Hard to believe it's been two days since the ceremony...)

Japanese: ‥‥ピピピピピピ‥‥
English: ...*beep beep beep*...

Japanese: （トランシーバーが！）
English: (The transceiver!)

Japanese: もしもし！　もしもしっ！
English: Hello!? Hello!?

Japanese: ‥‥これは電話ではありませんよ。
English: ...This is not a phone.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんは！ 彼女は‥‥ブジだろうな！
English: Maya! How is Maya!? You haven't hurt her, have you!?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、 要求は拒否されたようですね。
English: ...It seems you were not able to fulfill your end of the bargain, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ニュースを聞きました。
English: I have heard the news.

Japanese: ‥‥私のプレゼントも 意味がありませんでしたか‥‥。
English: So it would seem my present did you no good...

Japanese: ‥‥い、いけません！ 真宵さま！　真宵さまあああ！
English: N-Noooo! Mystic Maya! Mystic Mayaaaaaaaa!

Japanese: もう１日！　たのむ！ あと１日だけ、待ってくれ！
English: One more day! Please! All I ask is for one more day!

Japanese: かならず‥‥今度こそ、 無罪判決を‥‥
English: I... I'll get a not guilty verdict for sure this time! Please...

Japanese: やむを得ませんね。
English: I suppose if I must.

Japanese: 私としても、無罪判決が どうしても必要なのですよ。
English: I need that acquittal more than anything else, after all.

Japanese: ‥‥たのむ！　声を‥‥ 真宵ちゃんの声を聞かせてくれ！
English: ...Please... Please let Maya say something! I want to hear she's alright!

Japanese: そうザザ‥‥では‥‥ザザザ‥‥ ちょっとザザ‥‥ザザザザ‥‥
English: Alright... hissssss... then... hisssssss... ssssssssss... ssss... a little... sssssss...

Japanese: （‥‥なんだ！　急に 雑音が鳴り出したぞ‥‥）
English: (What is with this static all of a sudden...!)

Japanese: もしもし！　もしもし！
English: Hello!? Hello!?

Japanese: どうやらザザ‥‥のぐあ‥‥ザ‥‥
English: It seems... ssssss... hisss... sssss... bad... sssssss... hisssssssssss... ssssssssss...

Japanese: わるいザザザザ‥‥
English: connect...sssssssssssss...

Japanese: （くそっ！　トランシーバーが コワれたのか‥‥？）
English: (Dammit! Did the transceiver just suddenly break...!?)

Japanese: ザザ‥‥シツレイ‥‥ザザザザ‥‥
English: ssssssssss... 'll excuse me... hisssssssssssssssss...

Japanese: い、今のは‥‥？
English: Wh-What happened...?

Japanese: わからない。いきなり ガーピー言いだしたんだ。
English: I don't know. All of a sudden, it became nothing but static.

Japanese: ああ‥‥真宵さま‥‥真宵さま！
English: Aaaah... Mystic Maya! Mystic Maya!

Japanese: （どうしていきなり コワれちまったんだ‥‥？）
English: (Why did the transceiver suddenly break like that...?)

Japanese: （だれか‥‥キカイに詳しそうな 人に、相談したほうがいいな）
English: (I should probably have an electronics expert look at it...)

Japanese: （‥‥それも、なるべく早く！）
English: (The sooner the better!)

Japanese: 冷蔵庫の中は‥‥ いろいろ入ってるな。
English: There are all sorts of things in this refrigerator...

Japanese: ‥‥ニンジンジュースと‥‥ トマトジュースの空きビンです。
English: The carrot juice bottle and the tomato juice bottle are both empty...

Japanese: 空きビン‥‥？ （捨てるのがメンドウだったのか）
English: Empty...? (It's too much of a hassle to throw them away, I guess.)

Japanese: 野菜ジュースばっかりだね。 カラダに気をつかってたのかな。
English: They're all vegetable juices. I guess he must've been a real health nut.

Japanese: ‥‥あ。キムチとケチャップと イチゴジャムもありました。
English: ...Oh! There's a beet, some ketchup, and a bottle of strawberry jam too!

Japanese: ‥‥ただ赤い色が好きな だけかもしれないなあ。
English: Maybe red was his favorite color...?

Japanese: クマさんが ひしめいています！
English: Wow, there are a lot of bears!

Japanese: （目覚まし時計やら置物やら ぬいぐるみやらプラモデルやら）
English: (Alarm clock ones, collector's editions, stuffed teddies, plastic models...)

Japanese: （圧倒的な光景だな‥‥）
English: (It's pretty overwhelming... Is there a kind of bear he doesn't have...?)

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、ゴミ箱の中にも つっこまれてますけど‥‥。
English: There's even a few in the trash can...

Japanese: うーん‥‥本人は、あまり クマ好きじゃないのかも。
English: Yeah... I get the feeling maybe the guy didn't really like bears.

Japanese: クマさん、カワイソウです‥‥
English: Poor teddies...

Japanese: いやーホント、クマったもんだ。 ‥‥なーんて。
English: It's hard to "bear" with all these problems... Growl!

Japanese: あひゃ。あひゃらひゃら。あひゃ！ ひゃひゃひゃらあひゃあひゃ。ひゃ
English: Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: （こないだの事件‥‥ある種、 トラウマになっちまったな）
English: (I don't think I want to "bear" with the trauma the last case caused me...)
What's wrong, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: もう！ こんなにちらかして‥‥
English: Hmph! It's so messy here!

Japanese: （ホント、キレイ好きだな 春美ちゃんは‥‥）
English: (Pearls really likes things neat and tidy, I take it...)

Japanese: それにしても、見たことのない キカイがいっぱいありますね。
English: There are a lot of electronic things in here that I've never seen before.

Japanese: ね。なるほどくん。 教えてください！
English: Hey, Mr. Nick! Tell me what they are, pretty please?

Japanese: そこにあるのがウデ時計だよ。 手首につけるんだ。
English: OK. That over there is a "watch". You wear it on your wrist.

Japanese: ‥‥知ってます。
English: I know what a watch is...

Japanese: （‥‥しまった。相手は 真宵ちゃんじゃないんだった）
English: (Oops. For a second there, I forgot I was talking with Pearls, not Maya...)

Japanese: トランクだ。いろんなものが ギュウと詰めこまれている。
English: It's a suitcase. There are so many things in it that I bet it barely closes.

Japanese: 着替えにドライヤー、 ヒゲそりに電卓、か‥‥。
English: There's his clothes, a dryer, an electric shaver, a calculator...

Japanese: 旅行に関係ないものが まじっていますね。
English: Do all stars pack too much stuff like Mr. Corrida?

Japanese: この中に‥‥トノサマンが 入っていたのですね。
English: So, the Nickel Samurai's costume was in here?

Japanese: そうだね。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: そんなことまでして、 オートロさんの悪口を言おうと‥‥
English: I can't believe Mr. Corrida went so far, just to say bad things about Mr. Engarde...

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥“記者会見”だけど。
English: W-Well, it was a "press conference", so he had to go in costume.

Japanese: オートロさんとイサオさんは、 友だちなのではないですか？
English: But weren't Mr. Engarde and Mr. Corrida friends?

Japanese: 友だちじゃないよ。 “ライバル”だから。
English: They weren't friends. They couldn't be friends because they were rivals.

Japanese: “強敵”と書いて“とも”と 読むのが、正しいライバルです！
English: So, a "rival" is someone who is a "strong enemy"!?

Japanese: （春美ちゃん、アツいな‥‥）
English: (Pearls is really fired up over this... And I don't have an answer for her.)

Japanese: キレイなグラスだな。 中身は‥‥トマトジュースか。
English: It's a beautiful wine glass, and there's... tomato juice in it.

Japanese: わたくし、ニガテです ‥‥トマトジュース。
English: Ewww. Tomato juice. I don't really like it much.

Japanese: テーブルにボトルがあるね。 きっとあれだよ。
English: There's a bottle of it on the table over there. That's probably where this came from.

Japanese: これがウワサの “ベッド”というものですか‥‥。
English: So that's a bed...

Japanese: う、うん。ウワサのね。
English: Yup. It's big, but it's a bed.

Japanese: きゃあ！　なるほどくん！ ふかふかですッ！
English: Aaah! Mr. Nick! It's sooooo soft!

Japanese: （大事件みたいだ‥‥）
English: (How easily children are entertained.)

Japanese: イサオさん、 食事したみたいだね。
English: It looks like Mr. Corrida had dinner that night.

Japanese: このビン‥‥ トマトジュースです。
English: This bottle... It's tomato juice.

Japanese: （授賞式でゴチソウが たくさん並んでいたけど‥‥）
English: (We had a lot of food at the award show that night...)

Japanese: （スターって、 ハラがへるのかなあ）
English: (But I wonder if the stars had gone onstage after only eating a meager meal like this...)

Japanese: 化粧品のビンが 散乱している。
English: Bottles of cosmetics are scattered all over the floor.

Japanese: ‥‥ここで、イサオさんは 犯人と争ったのだろう。
English: This is probably where Mr. Corrida fought his assailant.

Japanese: この、ガラスのカケラが‥‥
English: These glass shards...

Japanese: 霧緒さんが落とした 花ビン、だね。
English: They're probably from the glass vase Ms. Andrews knocked over.

Japanese: 事件当夜のこと、もう一度だけ、 カクニンさせてください。
English: Please talk to me about the night of the murder just one more time.

Japanese: 裁判所でタップリ話したろ！
English: I talked about it plenty at the trial!

Japanese: あのインチキカメラマンの ラクガキにダマされちゃってサ！
English: I was fooled, tricked... DECEIVED by that fraud of a photographer and her note!

Japanese: アホヅラぶらさげて、このへんを ウロつきまわってたんだョ！
English: She was loitering around here with that imbecilic look on her face!

Japanese: “アホヅラぶらさげて”‥‥と。
English: "With that imbecilic look on her face"... OK, got it.

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっとアンタ！
English: N-Now hold on a second there you little pipsqueak!

Japanese: メモするんなら、もっと いいように書いてちょうだい！
English: If you're going to take notes, at least make me sound better than that!

Japanese: あ。かしこまりました。
English: Oh, alright.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Now I've seen everything...)

Japanese: でも、オバチャンも シゴトがあったからね。
English: But you know, I was working that night too, doing my job, minding my own business.

Japanese: ずっと見張っていたワケじゃ ないケド。
English: So it's not like I had time to waste standing around here the whole night.

Japanese: 亡くなったイサオさんのこと、 聞かせてもらえますか。
English: I was wondering if you could tell me a bit more about Mr. Corrida.

Japanese: 人気ナンバー・ワンだったね。 ‥‥特にオバチャンの中では。
English: He was the most popular star, you know... Especially where it counts -- in my book!

Japanese: オートロさんの人気を ねたんでいた、とか‥‥。
English: But I heard that he was lagging behind in the polls against Mr. Engarde...

Japanese: う。ま、まあ最近はちょっと、 アレだったけどサ。
English: Um, w-well, that's just a recent thing. Bad luck and all that, you know.

Japanese: でも！　ホントに これからだったんだョ！
English: But! He was going to become an even bigger star than he used to be!

Japanese: ホレ、ごらん！ このプレゼントの山！
English: Look! Just look at this mountain of presents!

Japanese: みーんな、ファンのコたちの キモチなんだョ！
English: It's a show of the mountain of feelings all his fans had for him!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥ たいしたもんですね。
English: Yeah, the mountain is pretty big, and certainly nothing to shake a stick at.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん？
English: ...Mr. Nick?

Japanese: ん？　どうしたの。
English: Hm? What is it, Pearls?

Japanese: この、プレゼント。 クマさんばっかり‥‥ですね。
English: The presents. They're all bears... right?

Japanese: （‥‥というか、クマ以外のものは １つもないぞ‥‥）
English: (She's got a point... There isn't a single thing here that isn't a bear...)

Japanese: ファンのプレゼントですけど ‥‥クマばっかりですね。
English: All of Mr. Corrida's presents from his fans seem to be... bears...

Japanese: ああ。イサオちゃんの イメージキャラクターだからね。
English: Oh, that's because you can't think of Juan without thinking about bears!

Japanese: クマさんが‥‥ですか。
English: Bears...? Why bears?

Japanese: いやなに。イサオちゃん、 修業時代にクマと素手で戦ってサ。
English: You don't know!? When my dear Juan was training, he fought barehanded with a bear.

Japanese: どうしても勝負がつかなくてネ。 それ以来、親友になったんだって。
English: He refused to give in and let the bear win, but after the fight, they became friends.

Japanese: ‥‥わあ。 ステキなおはなしですね。
English: Wow... What a heartwarming story.

Japanese: ホラ。青春ドラマであるだろ。 河原に寝ころんだふたりが、
English: Look, it's just like in those young people's dramas. I can see those two tuckered out,

Japanese: “ヘッ‥‥オマエ、やるな” “オマエもな”っていう、アレ。
English: down by a river going, "Heh... You... You sure can fight..." "You too, bub. You too."

Japanese: クマとそれをやったんですか。
English: D-Did all that really happen?

Japanese: 自伝に書いてあったョ。
English: It's in his biography, bub.

Japanese: （ゼッタイ、ウソだ！）
English: (What a load of crock!)

Japanese: それ以来、ファンのプレゼントは クマになったワケ。
English: So ever since then, fans have been giving him bears as presents.

Japanese: クマ、ねえ‥‥。
English: Yeah... Nice... Bears...

Japanese: アンタにやる情報なんて ないョ！
English: I don't have anything to say to delinquents like you!

Japanese: （うう‥‥見向きも しないぞ‥‥）
English: (Grrr... She's clamming up like the old clam she is...)

Japanese: おねがいします！ なんでもいいですから！
English: Please! Anything would be helpful!

Japanese: じゃ、オバチャンのスリーサイズ 教えてやろうか？
English: Well then, how about I tell you my measurements?

Japanese: ‥‥それはいいです。
English: ...Um... No, that's okay... Really.

Japanese: なるほどくん、人気が ないのですね‥‥
English: She really doesn't like you, does she Mr. Nick?

Japanese: メンボクない。
English: I know, I know...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 藤見野　イサオの控え室
English: March 22 Gatewater Hotel Corrida's Hotel Room

Japanese: おッ！　おそいッス！
English: Hey! You're finally here, pal!

Japanese: す、すみません。
English: S-Sorry to keep you waiting.

Japanese: それで‥‥“たんちき”のほうは？
English: Do you have the... um... "bug sweeper"?

Japanese: いや、その‥‥それが‥‥
English: Um, well, you see...

Japanese: 取り押さえられちまったッス。 ‥‥いきなり、署の入り口で。
English: I got busted trying to sneak in, pal. Then suddenly, I'm staring at the precinct doors.

Japanese: だから、持ち出せなかったッス。 ‥‥警察の探知機。
English: ...From the outside, I mean. So yeah... I couldn't get one of the police bug sweepers...

Japanese: ナニやってるんですか！ それがなくちゃあ‥‥
English: What do you mean you couldn't get it!? We need that item!

Japanese: ‥‥いやいや。いちおう ベツの探知機を持ってきたッス。
English: Hey, hey, calm down, pal. I didn't say I didn't get one, just not the police's.

Japanese: わあ。これがウワサの‥‥
English: Wow... So this is a "bug sweeper"...

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、ちょっと ボロっちいですね。
English: It looks a little... broken.

Japanese: コレ、小学生のころ、 工作の時間に作ったッス。
English: Hey, this was made when I was in elementary school, pal.

Japanese: え。‥‥だれが？
English: Oh? By who?

Japanese: モチロン、自分ッス。 ‥‥なつかしいッス。
English: Me, of course! Ah, seeing this sure brings back memories...

Japanese: バカにしちゃダメッス！ たしかに見た目はボロいッスが‥‥
English: Hey, don't look down on it, pal! Sure, it looks a little run down...

Japanese: コイツには、自分の マゴコロがこもっているッス！
English: But I put my heart and soul into building this puppy here!

Japanese: まごころ‥‥
English: Your heart and soul...?

Japanese: ちゃーんと、動くはずッス。 ただ‥‥
English: It'll work! Trust me, pal, it'll do the job! But...
But...?

Japanese: 周波数をセットすることが できないッスから、
English: But you can't set the sensitivity...

Japanese: あらゆる電波の発信源に、 それはビンカンに反応するッス。
English: So it's going to beep at anything that gives off electromagnetic waves.

Japanese: そのほうがいいのでは ないですか？
English: But isn't it better that way?

Japanese: はははははははははははははははは はははははははははははははははは
English: Oh ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho!

Japanese: ま。セッカク持ってきたッスから、 やってみるッス。
English: Well, anyway, since I brought it all this way, might as well give it a whirl, right pal?
(I'm getting that sinking feeling again...)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、説明するッス。
English: OK, now I'll tell you how to use this baby!

Japanese: この部屋にはおそらく、 盗聴器がしかけられているッス。
English: There's a listening device or some other sort of bug hidden in this room, pal.

Japanese: これから、それを 探し出すわけですね。
English: So we're going to find it, right?

Japanese: ‥‥まず、探知機の スイッチを入れるッス。
English: Right. Now, first, let's turn the sweeper on.

Japanese: 探知機にタッチして、イロイロな 場所を、よーくさがしてみるッス。
English: Next, touch the sweeper and take a real good look around the room with it, pal.

Japanese: 電波の強さは、《ＣＨＥＣＫ》 パネルを見ればわかるッス。
English: You can see how strong the radio waves are in an area by looking at the CHECK gauge.

Japanese: 強い電波をさぐりあてると、 パネルの表示が変わるッスから‥‥
English: Once you find something that's giving off strong waves, the gauge will change...

Japanese: そこでパネルをタッチすれば、 発信源を特定できるッス。
English: And when that happens, touch the gauge to really give the thing a long hard stare.

Japanese: 盗聴器以外にも、電波を発する モノはたくさんあるッスから‥‥
English: There's a lot of things here that's going to give off radio waves,

Japanese: アヤシイところは、 ゼンブ調べてみるッス！
English: so let's take a good look at anything and everything that seems suspicious, OK pal!?

Japanese: じゃ！　自分は部屋のそとで 見張りをしてるッスから‥‥、
English: Alright! I'm going to go stand outside and keep an eye out.

Japanese: 盗聴器を見つけたら、呼ぶッス！
English: Give me a yell if you find the bug. Got it, pal!?

Japanese: なるほどくん。‥‥そこは特に、 電波は出ていないようです。
English: There doesn't seem to be anything giving off radio waves there, Mr. Nick.

Japanese: 《ＣＨＥＣＫ》パネルの表示を よく見て、さがすのですよ。
English: Keep a careful eye on the CHECK gauge, and let's try again!

Japanese: （ちょっとヨワいんだよな ‥‥キカイは）
English: (This sweeper isn't exactly the best... Well, make do with what you have, right?)

Japanese: わあ。ごはんがおいしそうに たけています！
English: Wow, there's a really delicious loaf of bread in here!

Japanese: ‥‥事件があってから ずっと“保温中”か‥‥
English: Looks like it's been on "keep warm" all this time since the murder.

Japanese: けいじさん、おっしゃってました。 ゲンバは保温するものだと。
English: Well, Mr. Scruffy Detective always says, "Got to keep the trail and crime scene warm."

Japanese: （それは“保存”だと 思うけど‥‥）
English: (I think the "keep warm" in that case is a little more metaphorical...)

Japanese: ラジオがつけっぱなしに なっています。
English: The radio is on and playing something.

Japanese: あ！　『こども電話質問ばこ』の コーナーですよ！
English: Oh! It's "Kids' Question Corner"!

Japanese: “せんせい！　‥‥ちきゅうは どうして、まるいんですか？”
English: "Professor! Professor! Why is the Earth round?"

Japanese: どうしてですか？ なるほどくん。
English: Yes, why is it, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: しばらく聞いてていいよ。 ラジオ。
English: Why don't you listen to the radio program a little more, Pearls?

Japanese: ‥‥ただの電卓だね。
English: It's a plain old calculator.

Japanese: “５０”って 表示されています。
English: It says "50" on it.

Japanese: おこづかいを 計算していたのでしょうか。
English: Maybe he was calculating his allowance?

Japanese: （霊媒師のこづかいは ５０円なのか‥‥）
English: (A whole 50 cents?? Um, maybe if he was a spirit medium...)

Japanese: ノートパソコンか‥‥ 盗聴器はついてないね。
English: There's no listening device in that notebook computer, huh.

Japanese: のーとぱそこん‥‥というのは、 いったい、どのような‥‥？
English: Umm... What's a "notebook computer"...?

Japanese: “ノート”はわかるよね？
English: Do you know what a "notebook" is?

Japanese: ええ！　はい！ ‥‥それはもう！
English: Yes! It's a small book with paper that you can write on! ...So?

Japanese: それがぜいたくに なったのが、コレなんだ。
English: Well, that thing is like a notebook in a way. It's basically a small laptop.

Japanese: はあ‥‥なるほど。 ゼイタク、ですか‥‥
English: Umm... Mr. Nick... What's a "laptop"...?

Japanese: コイツはドライヤーだな。 あやしいところは‥‥ないね。
English: And here we have a dryer... Nothing unusual here, I think.

Japanese: どらいやー‥‥
English: A dryer...?

Japanese: あ！　テレビのそばで使うと、 ザザッてなりますよね！
English: Oh! If you use it next to a TV, it'll make the screen look all weird, right?

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そうだね。
English: ...Y-Yeah.

Japanese: それがウワサの “でんぱしょうがい”なのですね！
English: And when that happens, it's called "electromagnetic interference", right!?

Japanese: お。よくおぼえてるね。
English: Hey, good memory, Pearls!

Japanese: わたくし、もう一生 忘れませんとも！
English: I will never forget it for the rest of my life!

Japanese: これはなんですか？
English: What's this?

Japanese: 小型のビデオカメラだよ。 ‥‥盗聴器はしかけられてないな。
English: It's a small video camera. ...No listening device in this gizmo.

Japanese: 最近は、なんでも“小型”が はやっていますね、なるほどくん。
English: Everyone's trying to make everything smaller and smaller lately, aren't they Mr. Nick?

Japanese: そうだね。
English: That's what it seems like.

Japanese: でも！　わたくしは 大きくなるつもりですから。
English: But! I want to grow bigger and bigger!

Japanese: （それなら、野菜ばっかりじゃ なくて、ニクも食べなきゃ‥‥）
English: (Well, eating only vegetables isn't going to help you there. You have to eat meat too...)

Japanese: 電気スタンドだ。 ‥‥盗聴器は、ない。
English: Lamp, check. Listening device... nope.

Japanese: この部屋、電気スタンドが いっぱいありますね。
English: There are a lot of lamps in this room, aren't there Mr. Nick?

Japanese: そうだね。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: しかも、ゼンブ つけっぱなし。
English: And they're all on.

Japanese: ダメですよ、なるほどくん。 このようなことをしては。
English: You shouldn't do that, Mr. Nick. Don't you know that's wasteful?

Japanese: ‥‥き、気をつけます。
English: ...Ah, yeah... I'll be more conscientious from now on. Sorry.

Japanese: “盗聴器”といえば、やっぱり 電話が定番だよな。
English: Well, the phone is the most common place for a "listening device", I'd say.

Japanese: とりあえず、受話器を分解 してみよう。
English: But, let's take the receiver apart first before we get ahead of ourselves here.

Japanese: わあ‥‥。キカイにも 強いのですね、なるほどくん。
English: Wow, you know a lot about electronics, don't you Mr. Nick?

Japanese: うん。こわすのはね、 トクイなんだ。
English: Yeah, I know tons. Especially when it comes to taking them apart. It's my specialty.

Japanese: （なおすのはイトノコ刑事に まかせよう‥‥）
English: (I'll leave the fixing part up to Gumshoe...)

Japanese: それで‥‥いかがですか？ “とうちょうき”のほうは。
English: So...? Is there a "listening device" in there?

Japanese: ‥‥ないや。 （電話だと思ったんだけどなあ）
English: ...No. (And I really thought it had to be in the phone too...)

Japanese: どうやら‥‥目覚まし時計 みたいだな。
English: Well, it certainly looks like an alarm clock.

Japanese: ‥‥どうされたのですか？ ムズカシイかおをして。
English: What's wrong? Why do you look troubled?

Japanese: “目覚まし時計”と“盗聴器”の 組み合わせがね。
English: I just can't imagine the listening device being inside this alarm clock.

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと、ムカシの事件を 思い出しちゃって‥‥。
English: It just, um, sort of reminded me of something that happened a long time ago...

Japanese: はあ‥‥
English: Oh...

Japanese: ‥‥とりあえず、盗聴器は しかけられてないね。
English: Well, anyway, it looks like the listening device isn't in here.

Japanese: さすがに、テレビに盗聴器 ってことはないだろうな。
English: I don't really think the listening device is in the TV of all places.

Japanese: テレビ、つけっぱなしですね。 ‥‥アメリカの映画ですよ。
English: Looks like the TV was left on, and it's now showing an old samurai movie.

Japanese: そうだね。西部劇かな。
English: Yeah. This channel plays all sorts of international movies, as well as domestic ones.

Japanese: いつも思うのですが‥‥
English: You know, every time I watch one of these old movies, I always think,

Japanese: 日本語がおじょうずですよね。 アメリカのスターさんたちって。
English: "Wow, these Japanese stars are really good at English!"
...Um, yeah.

Japanese: わたくしも、大きくなったら 英語をべんきょうしたいです！
English: When I grow up, I want to study Japanese!

Japanese: （ここは‥‥ユメを コワさないほうがいいのかな）
English: (I should probably keep my mouth shut here, and not destroy her dream...)

Japanese: ‥‥盗聴器はないね。 このエアコンには。
English: Well, the listening device isn't in the air conditioner.

Japanese: まあ！　ホコリだらけですね。 エアコンのフィルター‥‥
English: Aah! Yuck! This air filter is covered in dust and dirt...

Japanese: そ、そうだね。
English: Y-Yeah...

Japanese: 洗っておきましょうよ！ なるほどくん！
English: Come on, Mr. Nick! Let's wash it!

Japanese: （キレイ好きは これだから困るんだよな‥‥）
English: (I wonder if being a neat freak makes even the tiniest dust bunny look colossal...?)

Japanese: ‥‥何か？
English: ...What?

Japanese: い、いや、ベツに。
English: O-Oh, nothing.

Japanese: これはなんですか？ ポットに似てますけど‥‥。
English: What's this? It sort of looks like a hot water pot, but...

Japanese: ああ。ポットみたいな もんだね。
English: Oh, well, it's kind of like a hot water pot, I guess.

Japanese: お湯のかわりに コーヒーが出てくるだけだよ。
English: But instead of hot water, coffee comes out.

Japanese: えええっ！　そそ、そんな ポットがあるのですか！
English: Really!? Th-This pot can do that!?

Japanese: あの。オレンジジュースが 出てくるポットは‥‥？
English: Um, is there a pot that orange juice comes out of...?

Japanese: ‥‥たぶん、 それはないと思うよ。
English: I don't think there's anything like that, Pearls. Sorry.

Japanese: あ。このポット、 お湯がなくなりかけています。
English: Oh, the water in this hot water pot has run out!

Japanese: わたくし、入れかえて おきますね！
English: I'll go get more water for it!

Japanese: う、うん。いいことだね。
English: O-OK. Sounds good.

Japanese: （“盗聴器を探している”という ジジツを忘れそうになるな‥‥）
English: (Looks like she's forgotten all about looking for the listening device...)

Japanese: これは‥‥冷蔵庫ですね。
English: This is a... a refrigerator, right?

Japanese: さすがに、こんなモノに盗聴器は しかけないだろうなあ。
English: I really don't think the listening device is in something like this.

Japanese: 冷蔵庫の中は、野菜ジュース ばっかりですからね！
English: Because it's filled with nothing but healthy vegetable juices, right!?

Japanese: そ、そうだね。 （なんの関係があるんだろう‥‥）
English: Uh, yeah, sure. (What does that have to do with listening devices?)

Japanese: ‥‥携帯電話か。 盗聴器は‥‥ないな。
English: A cell phone... Nope, no bugs in here.

Japanese: けいたい‥‥でんわ、ですか？
English: A "cell phone"...?

Japanese: え！　まままさか、 携帯電話を知らないの？
English: Wh-What!? Don't tell me you don't know what a cell phone is!

Japanese: ‥‥すみません。 見たことないです‥‥
English: ...I'm sorry. I've never seen one before...

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥）
English: (Now that she mentions it,)

Japanese: （倉院の里、携帯電話の電波が 届かないんだった‥‥）
English: (my cell phone couldn't get any reception while I was staying in Kurain Village...)

Japanese: （春美ちゃん、里から出たことが ないらしいから‥‥）
English: (And Pearls has never lived outside of that village, so...)

Japanese: （ムリもない、と言えなくも ないけど‥‥）
English: (Well, I guess I can't say it's impossible to live without one...)

Japanese: テレビのリモコンだね。 ‥‥盗聴器はしかけられてないよ。
English: It's the TV's remote control. But it doesn't look like the listening device is in here.

Japanese: あの‥‥わたくし、 気がついたのですけど‥‥
English: Um, so I was thinking...

Japanese: “りもこん”やら“ぱそこん”、 “えあこん”などなど。
English: I wonder if a TV remote works on other things... Like...

Japanese: この“こん”というのは どういう意味なのですか？
English: Could I make you change your expressions like TV channels? Zap!
H-Hey!

Japanese: （ホンキで説明したら、 夜が明けてしまいそうだな‥‥）
English: (Hmm, but if I could... Ooh, the people I would give the ol' mute button to!)

Japanese: ま、真宵ちゃんに聞こうよ。 ‥‥明日。
English: Well, I don't think it's going to work on me... Why don't we try it on Maya tomorrow, OK?

Japanese: わかりました！
English: OK!

Japanese: まあ。愛らしいクマさん‥‥
English: Ah, what a lovely bear...

Japanese: ガオオオオオオオオオオッ！
English: *GROOOOOOOOOOOOWL!*

Japanese: きゃあああああああああっ！
English: AAAAAAAAAAAAAH!!

Japanese: あ。コイツは、今はやりの クマ型ロボットのおもちゃだね。
English: Ah, this must be one of those fancy bear-shaped toy robots.

Japanese: ろろ、ろぼっとですか！ これが！　ウワサの！
English: I-It's a robot!? It's a REAL robot!?

Japanese: ううう、うん。ウワサの。
English: Y-Yeah, it's a real one.

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Mr. Nick!

Japanese: な、ナニ？
English: Y-Yes?

Japanese: ば‥‥馬力は！　このろぼっと、 何万馬力ですかっ！
English: H-How many horse-powers is it? How many horsies!?

Japanese: ばりき‥‥？
English: Horsies...?

Japanese: さ、さあ‥‥。 クマ‥‥だしね、コイツは。
English: Um... Well, look, it's a bear, so... uh...

Japanese: ううう‥‥。
English: Um...

Japanese: これ‥‥クマさんの ぬいぐるみ、ですよね‥‥。
English: This is... This is just a giant stuffed teddy bear, right?

Japanese: ‥‥今までで、 イチバン強く反応しています。
English: It's the biggest one I have ever seen!

Japanese: おッ！　見つけたッスか？ 盗聴器‥‥
English: Hey! So did you guys find it yet? The listening device, I mean...

Japanese: いいえ。ただ、 このクマさんの目が‥‥
English: No, not yet. But this bear's eye is...

Japanese: ほっほお‥‥
English: Let's see, let's see...

Japanese: ただのぬいぐるみに、 強い電波反応‥‥
English: A perfectly normal stuffed bear with some really strong radio waves...

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ 見つけたみたいッスね。
English: Sounds like you found the device to me, pal.

Japanese: ちょっと、コイツの目玉を えぐり出してみるッス。
English: Let's dig this big fella's eye out and see what we've got.

Japanese: い、いけません！ そんな、ランボーな‥‥
English: N-No! You can't! Such... Such a violent act...

Japanese: えいっ！
English: Oomph! *rip!*

Japanese: きゃあっ！
English: Noooooooooo!

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥！
English: Th-That's!

Japanese: 小型のカメラ‥‥ッスね。 まだあるッス。
English: It's a miniature camera... And it looks like there's more.

Japanese: こっちは‥‥、 トランスミッターとタイマー。
English: There's a... transmitter, and a timer.

Japanese: ‥‥なにミッターですって？
English: A whattawhatta-mitter?

Japanese: トランスミッター、ッス。
English: A transmitter, pal.

Japanese: はああ。 “はいてく”ですねえ‥‥。
English: Oh... Is this more of that "high tech" stuff...?

Japanese: ‥‥わかったッス！
English: ...I got it!

Japanese: な、なんですか‥‥？
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: ちょっと、この盗撮カメラ、 借りるッス！
English: Hey, pal. Let me borrow this mini camera for a bit!

Japanese: ど、どうするんですか？
English: Wh-What are you going to do?

Japanese: 今から電気屋を回って、 これを買った人間を探すッス！
English: I'm going to go around to the electronic shops and see if I can find out who bought this!

Japanese: ム‥‥ムリですよ！ もうそろそろ、９時ですよ？
English: B-But that's impossible! I mean, it's already 9 PM!

Japanese: まかせるッス。一晩中かかっても 探し出して見せるッス。
English: Leave it to me! Even if I have to search all night, I'll find your man, pal!

Japanese: 《盗撮カメラとトランスミッター》 をイトノコ刑事にあずけた。
English: Spy Camera and Transmitter given to Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: うおおおおお！　捜査ッス！
English: Oh yeah, baby! It's investigating time!

Japanese: 血が鳴るッス！ ウデがさわぐッス！
English: I'm on fire, pal! My fingers are itching to go!

Japanese: ソーサッスゥゥゥゥゥゥゥゥゥゥ！
English: Yeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeah!

Japanese: ‥‥行ってしまいました。
English: ...He's gone.

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。
English: ...Yeah.

Japanese: でも。けいじさん、いいヒトです。
English: But Mr. Scruffy Detective sure is a nice man.

Japanese: 真宵さまのために、あんなに 必死になってくれて‥‥。
English: He's pushing himself so hard, all for Mystic Maya's sake...

Japanese: ‥‥やってることは ムチャクチャだがな。
English: ...You always manage to do things in the most ineffective ways.
(Ack!)

Japanese: シツレイする。 ‥‥話は聞かせてもらった。
English: You'll have to excuse me. I heard your conversation just now.
E-Edgeworth! What are you doing here!?

Japanese: とりあえず真宵くん捜索班の 編成・配備を完了した。
English: A rescue team has been created and deployed.

Japanese: 楽観はできないが‥‥ 一歩、前進だ。
English: I can't say I'm optimistic... but we have to move forward, one step at a time.

Japanese: そ、そうか‥‥。 すまないな、御剣。
English: I-I see... Thanks.

Japanese: 礼にはおよばない。 彼女が発見されないかぎりは。
English: Don't thank me yet. We still have to find her.

Japanese: ほお‥‥、このぬいぐるみに 盗撮カメラが‥‥？
English: Hmm... So, there was a spy camera hidden inside this stuffed animal, huh?

Japanese: 運がよかったな、成歩堂。
English: You are one lucky man, Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥おじょうちゃん。 このクマを知っているだろうか？
English: Do you know this stuffed bear, little girl?

Japanese: ‥‥知りません！
English: Um... I have no idea!

Japanese: フッ‥‥キラわれたものだな。
English: Hmph... Of course not.

Japanese: こいつを作ったメーカーは、 海外の高級ブランドだ。
English: The maker of this bear is a very expensive, luxury brand from overseas.

Japanese: すべてハンドメイドで、 日本への出荷数も、とても少ない。
English: It's completely hand-made, and there are very few that are exported here.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと？
English: Wh-What?

Japanese: あわて者の刑事クンが持ち出した カメラなど、手がかりにはならん。
English: The camera and transmitter that scatterbrained detective took with him are dead ends.

Japanese: どんな経路でも入手できるからな。 しかし‥‥コイツはちがう。
English: Things like those can be bought anywhere... However, this bear is different.

Japanese: 入手ルートを追うなら、 このクマを選ぶべきだった。
English: By tracking how it got into this country, this bear can tell us who the buyer is.

Japanese: そ、そうなのですか？ なるほどくん‥‥。
English: C-Can you really do that!? Mr. Nick, can he really?

Japanese: さ、さあ‥‥。
English: W-Well, I guess so...

Japanese: ‥‥午後９時。まだ間に合うな。
English: Hmm... It's 9 PM. I think I can still make it in time.

Japanese: コイツは私が引き受けよう。
English: I'll be taking this for now.

Japanese: キミは、他に するべきことがあるはずだ。
English: I'm sure you have other things you have to do.

Japanese: 証拠品《ぬいぐるみ》を 御剣にうばわれた。
English: Stuffed Bear snatched up by Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ。 またあとで会おう。
English: See you soon, Wright.

Japanese: ま‥‥、待て！
English: W-Wait!

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ。
English: What?

Japanese: なぜだ‥‥？ いったい、何を考えている！
English: Why are you doing this...?

Japanese: リカイできない人間に 説明するシュミはない。
English: I have no interest in explaining myself to someone who cannot comprehend.

Japanese: それより、成歩堂。
English: But besides that, Wright.

Japanese: 明日の法廷までに、 これだけは調べておくのだ。
English: Until court reconvenes tomorrow, you should concern yourself with this question:

Japanese: “藤見野　イサオを殺害したのは いったい、だれなのか‥‥？”
English: "Who was the person that murdered Juan Corrida?"
The "real killer"...

Japanese: まだ、華宮　霧緒だと 思っているのか？
English: Do you really still think it was Adrian Andrews?

Japanese: 正直‥‥よくわからない。
English: To be honest... I don't know anymore.

Japanese: 時間はまだ残されている。 ‥‥少しはな。
English: You still have a little time left...

Japanese: 真実を受け入れろ。 すべては、そこからだ。
English: Find the truth, Wright. Everything begins with the truth.

Japanese: （イサオさんを殺害した犯人‥‥ 霧緒さんの過去‥‥）
English: (Juan Corrida's real killer... Ms. Andrews' past...)

Japanese: （オートロさんの無罪判決を 要求する誘拐犯‥‥）
English: (The kidnapper whose sole condition is an acquittal for Mr. Engarde...)

Japanese: （そして、このカード。 虎狼死家　左々右エ門‥‥）
English: (And... this card. Shelly de Killer...)

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃん。 きみを助け出すには、
English: ...Maya. The only way I can save you now,

Japanese: 今夜中に‥‥すべての答えを 見つけ出すしかない！
English: is to find all the answers to this case tonight!

Japanese: 御剣の真意はわからないけど‥‥
English: I don't understand what your real intentions are, Edgeworth...

Japanese: お前の言うとおりだ。 ‥‥とにかく、やるしかない！
English: but as you said, all I can do for now is find the truth!

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: この、ちっちゃいのが ‥‥カメラなんですか？
English: So this tiny thing is a camera...?

Japanese: ピンホール型のＣＣＤカメラ。
English: Yup. It's a pinhole CCD camera, pal.

Japanese: 盗み撮りによく使われる、 高性能な超小型ビデオカメラッス。
English: It's a small, high-grade video camera mostly used in security systems.

Japanese: ビデオカメラ‥‥。
English: So it's a video camera...

Japanese: いっしょにセットされている バッテリーの電力で動くッス。
English: It runs on a battery, which comes with it in a set.

Japanese: でも‥‥ ビデオテープがありませんよ？
English: But... there's no video tape in this camera...

Japanese: ここにあるのは、カメラだけッス。
English: This is only the camera part here, pal.

Japanese: テープに録画するビデオレコーダー は‥‥別のところにあるッス。
English: The tape recorder with the tape inside it is somewhere else.

Japanese: 別のところ‥‥ですか？
English: Somewhere else?

Japanese: 映像を電波に変えて、 そのレコーダーに発信するッス。
English: The footage is changed into radio waves and then it's sent to that recorder.

Japanese: まるで‥‥ テレビ局みたいですね。
English: So... It's sort of like a TV broadcast, isn't it?

Japanese: まさに、そのとおりッス。
English: Hey, you know, you're right!

Japanese: 証拠品《盗撮カメラ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Spy Camera added to the Court Record.

Japanese: トランスミッター‥‥って、 なんですか？
English: So... what is a "transmitter"?

Japanese: カメラがとらえた 映像を、送信するキカイッス。
English: It's a device that sends footage the camera took to a specific destination.

Japanese: まあ‥‥盗聴器のビデオ版、 みたいなものッス。
English: It's like a video version of a listening device, pal.

Japanese: 小さな時計みたいなモノに つながってますけど‥‥。
English: It looks like it's attached to a small, clock-like thing...

Japanese: これは、タイマーッス。
English: Oh, that's a timer, pal.

Japanese: カメラの作動する時間が セットされているッス。
English: You can set it to turn the camera on and record at a certain time with it.

Japanese: 時間が‥‥
English: You can set it for a certain time...?

Japanese: そッスね。タイマーによると‥‥ 午後８時から、１時間ッス。
English: Yup. Let's see... This looks like it was set to start at 8 PM and go for one hour.

Japanese: 午後８時‥‥ （授賞式が終わった時間だ！）
English: Eight PM? (That was the time the award ceremony ended!)

Japanese: 日付の指定はないッスから、 毎晩、カメラは作動してるッス。
English: There's no date set, so it's been recording every night, I'd guess.

Japanese: け、けいじさん！　ぬいぐるみは、 いつからここに‥‥？
English: M-Mr. Detective! How long has this bear been here...?

Japanese: たしか、事件当夜からあったッス。
English: Um, I'm pretty sure it's been here since the night of the murder.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥もしかして？
English: Then... Then maybe...

Japanese: この部屋で起こった殺人事件は‥‥ 撮影されていたことになるッス！
English: Maybe this camera caught the murder on tape!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: ぬいぐるみの置かれた 角度から考えて‥‥
