English: And if you think about the angle the bear is at...

Japanese: そのビデオには、犯行のすべてが 録画されているッス！
English: It's bound to have had a clear shot of the whole crime, pal!

Japanese: 証拠品《トランスミッター》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Transmitter added to the Court Record.

Japanese: このクマさんの目に‥‥カメラが。
English: So there was a camera... in this bear's eye...

Japanese: プレゼントに偽装して セットされていたんだ。
English: And it was disguised as a present.

Japanese: まちがいなく、事件当夜にも ここに置いてあったッス。
English: And I'm sure it was here on the night of the murder, pal.

Japanese: とても目立つぬいぐるみッス。 よくおぼえてるッス。
English: It's pretty big, so it stands out pretty well in my mind.

Japanese: でも。‥‥いったい、 だれがこんなものを？
English: But... who gave Mr. Corrida this present?

Japanese: それは‥‥わからねッス。
English: I, ah, don't know, pal.

Japanese: しかし！
English: But!

Japanese: 事件当夜、この部屋のようすを ビデオに記録したヤツがいるッス！
English: This means that someone out there's got a video of what happened here that night!

Japanese: それがだれなのか、 わからないのでしょうか‥‥。
English: Isn't there any way we can find out who that person is?

Japanese: ‥‥それはムリッス。
English: It's impossible, pal.

Japanese: 電波は、どこにいても 受信できるッスからねえ‥‥
English: Radio waves can be sent almost anywhere, so there's no real way to find out...

Japanese: うううう‥‥
English: Oh... *sniffle*

Japanese: （‥‥何か、 方法はないのか‥‥？）
English: (Is there really no way to find out...?)

Japanese: 証拠品《ぬいぐるみ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Stuffed Bear added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥コロシヤ　サザエモン っていう殺し屋‥‥ッスか？
English: So... the assassin's name is Shelly de Killer?

Japanese: ええ。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: サザエモン‥‥だから、 サザエのカード？
English: And because he's "Shelly", that's why he leaves a "shell" card?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes.

Japanese: はっはっはっはっ！
English: Ho ho ho ho!

Japanese: いやー、いくら自分が アレだからといっても‥‥
English: Oh, that's great. But you know, no matter how strange I may seem,

Japanese: さすがにそんなジョークは 通用しないッス。
English: at least my jokes don't make you cringe. Right, pal?

Japanese: （ホンキにしてもらえない。 ‥‥ムリもないか‥‥）
English: (Umm... Yeah, you keep on thinking that...)

Japanese: この装置は、どうやら 犯人が自分で作ったみたいッスね。
English: This device looks like it was made by the criminal themselves.

Japanese: 映像の送信だけなら、８０時間は カルいッス。
English: If it's only sending images, then I'd say it'd last about 80 hours, pal.

Japanese: けいじさん‥‥くわしいのですね。
English: You know a lot about these things, don't you Mr. Detective?

Japanese: もしかして‥‥盗撮が ごシュミなのですか？
English: Is spying on people a hobby of yours...?

Japanese: いやー、そんな。またまた。 テレるッス。
English: Well, no... I mean... You're making me embarrassed, putting me on the spot like this.

Japanese: （テレてる場合じゃ ないと思うけど‥‥）
English: (Why should he be embarrassed? Is there something I'm not getting here...?)

Japanese: ちっちゃいカメラですね。
English: It's a really tiiiiny camera.

Japanese: このサイズのバッテリーなら、 動作時間は３時間ていどッス。
English: A camera this size lasts about 3 hours with a fresh battery pack, pal.

Japanese: いったい、なんのために こんなものを‥‥？
English: But what would something like this be doing in...?

Japanese: そりゃ、殺人事件を 盗み撮りしようとしたッス。
English: Well, I'd say taking a secret video of the murder.

Japanese: （ということは‥‥）
English: (Which means...)

Japanese: （知ってたワケか‥‥ ここで殺人があることを‥‥！）
English: (Someone knew there was going to be a murder here!)

Japanese: とても愛らしい クマさんですね。
English: The bear is really lovely, isn't it?

Japanese: 毛皮もふわふわしていて、 おカオも上品だし‥‥
English: It's so soft and fuzzy, and the face is really well- made...

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥他のプレゼントと くらべても、よくできてるな）
English: (Well, I guess when you compare it to all the other presents, it is well-made.)

Japanese: まあ、事件の手がかりには ならないッスけどね。
English: Yeah, but I don't think the bear itself is a clue to this case at all.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: March 22 Police Station Criminal Affairs Dept.

Japanese: わあ。なんだかみなさん、 いそがしそうですね‥‥。
English: Wow. Everybody looks really busy with something or another...

Japanese: うん。‥‥明日の法廷に向けて、 証拠固めをしてるんだろうね。
English: Hmm... They're probably strengthening the evidence for tomorrow's trial.

Japanese: ‥‥おい！　早く 被害者のリストをまわしてくれ！
English: Hey! Hurry up with that, will ya!? Pass that victim's list around!

Japanese: ‥‥ムチャ言うな！ １００人以上いるんだぞ！
English: ...Now you're speaking nonsense! There's more than 100 people on there!

Japanese: ‥‥あの。なるほどくん？
English: Um, Mr. Nick...?

Japanese: オートロさんて、 大悪党だったのですか‥‥？
English: Is Mr. Engarde a really bad, terrible criminal...?

Japanese: どうやら、ベツの事件‥‥ みたいだね。
English: Actually, Pearls, never mind. It sounds like they're working on a different case...

Japanese: まあ、愛らしい。
English: Aww, it's kind of cute.

Japanese: なるほどくん。この お人形さんは‥‥？
English: Mr. Nick, what is this stuffed animal's name...?

Japanese: “タイホくん”だよ。
English: That's the "Blue Badger"!

Japanese: ワシが考えて、ワシが作った この署のマスコットさ。
English: It was my idea! I made it! It's this precinct's mascot, you know.

Japanese: そうなのですか‥‥
English: Oh, wow...

Japanese: コイツの人気を全国区にするのが、 ワシの最後の仕事だよ。
English: I'll get him assigned mascot of every police station, if it's the last thing I do!

Japanese: おしごと、がんばってください。
English: I hope you succeed in your mission, sir!

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “‥‥ハイ。この会社を選んだ 動機ですか？”
English: "...Very well. Now why do you want to work for our company?"

Japanese: “それはやはり、御社の将来性と 企業方針に共感したからです”
English: "Well, that would have to be because I feel I would like to help this company grow."

Japanese: なぜか、入社面接のイメージ・ トレーニングをしているようだ。
English: He must be doing image training for corporate interviews.

Japanese: 刑事課の課長さんだろう。
English: This must be the chief of the detectives here.

Japanese: ‥‥パソコンの画面を、 食いいるように見つめている。
English: He's glued to his computer screen.

Japanese: なにぃ‥‥！　“裁判所の前で、 狩魔検事が狙撃‥‥”
English: WHAT!? Prosecutor von Karma was shot in front of the courthouse!?

Japanese: い‥‥いったい、 ダレを撃ったんだッ！
English: Wh-Who did it!? Who shot her!?

Japanese: （‥‥イトノコさんでも 課長になれそうだな‥‥）
English: (I have a sneaking suspicion that even Gumshoe could do this guy's job...)

Japanese: 刑事たちの机がならんでいる。
English: These are the detectives' desks.

Japanese: パソコンに、資料。 ‥‥思ったより片づいているな。
English: There are computers and files on each one. Funny, they're a lot tidier than I expected.

Japanese: たぶん、あまり席にいることが ないんだろう。
English: I guess the detectives don't spend a lot of time at their desks.

Japanese: 婦人警官がビシッと 敬礼している。
English: A poster of a female police officer...

Japanese: ‥‥と思ったら、制服を着た アイドルのグラビアだった。
English: Wait, no. That's the latest "Babes in Uniform" calendar. My bad.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 堀田クリニック　ロビー
English: March 22 Hotti Clinic Reception

Japanese: （‥‥まさか、また来ることに なるとはなあ‥‥）
English: (...Never thought I'd ever come back to this place...)

Japanese: ん。ご面会かな？　ん。
English: Hmm, yes... Are you here to visit a patient? Hmm...

Japanese: あ。どうも‥‥。 ‥‥って、あなたはッ！
English: Ah, hi... ...Wait a second! You're...!

Japanese: ん。院長の堀田（ほった） ですわ。ほっほっ。
English: Hmm, yes... I'm Director Hotti... Hoh, hoh.

Japanese: おじさん、まだいたんですか！
English: Why are you still here!?

Japanese: ん。なんだ。お。なんだ。 院長センセに向かって。ん。ん。
English: Hmm, yes... What is it? Hmm... Can I help you? You can tell me... Hmm, yes...

Japanese: 先生ッ！
English: Director Hotti!

Japanese: み‥‥御剣‥‥
English: E-Edgeworth...

Japanese: ん。院長の堀田（ほった） ですわ。ほっほっ。
English: Hmm, yes... I'm Director Hotti... Hoh, hoh.

Japanese: ‥‥お。あんたは今朝の。 ん。どうしたかな。ん。
English: ...Oh, you're the man from this morning. Hmm, yes... What is it?... Uh huh...

Japanese: 先生。　メイは‥‥ 狩魔　冥の具合は？
English: Director. Franziska... How is Franziska von Karma?

Japanese: ム。シンパイいらんよ。ん。 なんつってもホラ。
English: Hmm... You don't need to worry. Hmm, yes... She's in good hands...

Japanese: ワシがね。このワシが ジキジキにね。ん。ヒヒ。
English: Because, you see... I'm personally taking good care of her... Hmm, yes... Hee hee.

Japanese: ん。ホレ。なんだ‥‥手術をさ。 やっといたから。
English: Hmm, yes... and that thing... that surgery... It went well.

Japanese: ‥‥礼を言わせていただく。
English: ...You have my gratitude.

Japanese: （どうやら、知らないみたいだな。 この“院長先生”のヒミツ‥‥）
English: (It looks like Edgeworth doesn't know about this "Director" and his secret...)

Japanese: かわいそうに。彼女、すっかり おびえてるみたいだな。ん。
English: She looked so pitiful; absolutely terrified. Hmm, yes...

Japanese: ムリもないね。ん。 テッポーだもん、相手は。
English: But I understand, hmm... Hmm, yes... Her opponent was a gun, after all. Uh huh.

Japanese: ワシがコッソリ忍びよったら、 『きゃあ』とか叫んじゃってさ。
English: And when I snuck up on her real secret-like, she would scream really loud. Hmm, yes.

Japanese: はあ。
English: I see.

Japanese: いやあ、カワイイね。もお。 ひっぱたかれちまったよ。ムチで。
English: Ah, but she's really cute too. When I'd do that, she'd whip me with her whip, uh huh...

Japanese: むせび泣いちゃったもん、ワシ。 もお。クセになりそうだわ。ん。
English: Boy did I cry like a baby. Hmm, yes... But I think I could get used to it. Hmm...

Japanese: んぎゃあああああああああああッ！
English: Hmaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: ‥‥病室に帰りなさいな。
English: Go back to your room.

Japanese: キビシーな、もう。キビシー。 冥ちゃんたら。
English: You're so mean, uh huh... So mean... my frisky Friska.

Japanese: でも、そこがイイ
English: But that's good too--

Japanese: んぎゃあああああああああああッ！
English: Hmaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ、ワシ。ん。そろそろ、 点滴の時間だから。ん。
English: OK, OK... I... Hmm, yes... It's time for my IV drops. Hmm, yes...

Japanese: 何かしら？　成歩堂　龍一。 その、手に持ったチューリップは。
English: And what are those tulips doing in your hand, Mr. Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: （やっぱり、来るんじゃなかった ‥‥おみまいなんて！）
English: (Argh! I knew I shouldn't have come here!)

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔検事‥‥
English: ...Ms. von Karma.

Japanese: 華宮　霧緒は、きみとの ヤクソクを信じきっていたんだぞ。
English: Adrian Andrews believed you when you said,

Japanese: “真実をしゃべらなければ、 王都楼が有罪になる”‥‥って。
English: "If you don't tell the truth of what really happened, then Engarde will be found guilty."

Japanese: ‥‥そ、それがどうかした？
English: And what does that have to do with me?

Japanese: そのせいで、彼女は 有罪になりかけたんだぞ！
English: Because of that, she is now in danger of being found guilty herself!

Japanese: きみの言うことを 最後まで信じたせいで‥‥
English: All because she believed in your words until the very end...

Japanese: それは、彼女が弱い人間だから。 私にはカンケイないわ。
English: That still has nothing to do with me. She's just a weak person, that's all.

Japanese: きみだって知っていた はずだ。霧緒さんは‥‥
English: But you had to know she was...

Japanese: いててェェッ！
English: OWWW!

Japanese: ‥‥そろそろ、病院の 面会時間はおしまいよ。
English: I think visiting hours here are about over.

Japanese: シツレイするわ。
English: So, if you'll excuse me.

Japanese: どうしたのでしょう？ 急にあわてて‥‥
English: What's wrong? Why did she suddenly cut you off...?

Japanese: （思いっきり、 たたかれ損だな‥‥）
English: (Probably because she thinks I had the advantage in that argument...)
(...Edgeworth...)

Japanese: ３月２２日　午後　７時　４分 堀田クリニック　ロビー
English: March 22, 7:04 PM Hotti Clinic Reception

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん？
English: Mr. Nick?

Japanese: ん？　‥‥あ、ああ。 （真宵ちゃんのこと、考えてた）
English: Hm? O-Oh yes, Pearls? (Got caught up in my thoughts about Maya's situation...)

Japanese: ミツルギ検事‥‥ 行ってしまわれましたよ。
English: Mr. Edgeworth has left, you know.

Japanese: （‥‥とにかく、オートロさんを 信じるしかない）
English: (I guess for now, I have no choice but to believe in Mr. Engarde.)

Japanese: （でも‥‥一度、話を聞いて おいたほうがいいな‥‥）
English: (But I think I should listen to his story one more time...)

Japanese: ‥‥よし！ ぼくたちも行こうか。
English: Alright! Let's get going too.

Japanese: はいっ！
English: OK!

Japanese: この病院の受付だけど‥‥ だれもいないな。
English: This is the clinic's reception desk, but it doesn't look like there's anyone at the counter.

Japanese: カベに、“今月のコトバ” が張りだされている。
English: On the wall is this month's "Word to the Wise":

Japanese: 『粉骨砕身』‥‥。
English: "Put your best foot forward."

Japanese: 外科病院に張りだすにしては、 ビミョーなコトバだな。
English: Putting this up at a general clinic like this is doesn't really mean much, does it...?

Japanese: 待合室だろうか。
English: This must be the waiting area.

Japanese: 気合いを入れて ソファを並べてるワリに、
English: It's got the feeling of one and there are sofas lined up...

Japanese: 患者は少ないみたいだ。
English: but I don't see all that many patients.

Japanese: ん。もう面会時間も おしまいだからね。ん。
English: Hmm, yes... well, visiting hours are over. That's why... Hmm...

Japanese: これからはホレ。深夜の診察が 始まるワケ。ワシの。ククク。
English: It's time for nightly consultations to begin... Mine, that is... Heh heh heh.

Japanese: （‥‥あのオヤジさん、だれかが 取りしまるべきでは‥‥？）
English: (...Isn't there anyone keeping an eye on this guy...?)

Japanese: 患者さんが リハビリテーションをしている。
English: This patient is undergoing rehabilitation.

Japanese: たしかあの患者、こないだ 来たときもあそこにいた。
English: Wasn't that patient standing in the same spot the last time you came to visit?

Japanese: ゼンゼン、快方に向かっている ようすがない。
English: Doesn't look like they've moved any closer to the reception desk, huh?

Japanese: ちゃんと治療しているのか！ このビョーインは！　ん。
English: Is this clinic really doing anything!? Is that patient really getting better!? Hmm...

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと！　カッテに しゃべらないでくださいよ！
English: Hey! Don't just cut into my monologue like that! I'm explaining things here!

Japanese: いや。スマン。ん。ゴメン。ん。
English: Ah, sorry. Hmm, yes... Most sorry... Yes... Uh, huh...

Japanese: （あの奥のドア‥‥ どこに通じてるんだろう？）
English: (I wonder where that door way down there leads to...?)

Japanese: あ。あれはな。 レントゲン室だよ。ん。
English: Oh, that's the X-ray room. Hmm, yes...

Japanese: な、なんでぼくの考えてる コト、見ぬいたんですか？
English: H-H-How did you know what I was thinking!?

Japanese: ん。そりゃ、ホレ。 レントゲン室だから。ん。
English: Hmm, yes... Well, it's... Because that's the X-ray room... Uh huh, yes...

Japanese: （いいのか？　このセンセイ‥‥）
English: (There is something very peculiar about this guy... Because I know it's not me...)

Japanese: 裁判所の前で撃たれたの。 ちょっと、右肩をね。
English: I was shot in front of the courthouse, in my right shoulder.

Japanese: フッ‥‥たいしたことじゃない。 こんなの、よくあるコト。
English: Hmph... It's no big deal. This sort of thing happens all the time.

Japanese: ホントは、そのまま法廷に 立つことだってできたのに。
English: I even had full intentions of running the trial this morning.

Japanese: そんな！ ‥‥ムチャです！
English: But! But that would've been too much!

Japanese: （さっきまで おびえてたクセに‥‥）
English: (Yeah! You looked like you were deathly scared until only a few minutes ago...)

Japanese: ‥‥それなのに、そこの検事さんが ムリヤリここへ連れてきたの。
English: But I was dragged here by THAT prosecutor.

Japanese: そこまでして、私の手がらを 横取りしたかったのかしらね。
English: He even went so far as to grab me by the wrist the whole way here.

Japanese: 肩にはまだ、弾丸が残っていた。 当然の処置だ。
English: It was the only logical course of action given the bullet was still lodged in your shoulder.

Japanese: ‥‥おかげで、
English: But with me doing so...

Japanese: キミのムチャな取り引きの シリぬぐいをするハメになったが。
English: I found myself having to clean up after you and that irresponsible deal you made.

Japanese: （“取り引き”‥‥）
English: (I think I know what "deal" he's referring to...)

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔検事。
English: Ms. von Karma.

Japanese: きみはきのう、霧緒さんと 取り引きをしただろう？
English: You made a deal with Ms. Andrews yesterday, didn't you?

Japanese: ‥‥なんのことかしら？
English: I don't know what you mean.

Japanese: 王都楼さんをカクジツに 有罪にするために、
English: In order to make sure you got your guilty verdict on Mr. Engarde,

Japanese: 今日の法廷で、 彼女に証言を拒否させた。
English: you told Ms. Andrews to not testify in court today.

Japanese: ‥‥さあ、知らないわ。 そんな証拠があるのかしら？
English: Hmph, I don't know what you're talking about. Do you have proof that I made such a deal?

Japanese: なんだって‥‥！
English: Y-You're denying it!?

Japanese: ‥‥運がよかったわね。 成歩堂　龍一。
English: It looks like you were lucky, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: 私が法廷に立っていれば、 この事件はもう、終わっていた。
English: If I had been in court today, this trial would already be over.

Japanese: 霧緒さんの“偽装”を かくしたまま、か？
English: All while hiding Ms. Andrews' own crime?

Japanese: ‥‥そんなことは問題じゃない。 偽装があろうとなかろうと‥‥
English: That isn't my problem. Whether she had tampered with evidence or not...

Japanese: 私のするべきことはただ１つ。 王都楼の有罪を立証することよ。
English: I have only one objective. To find Engarde guilty of murder.

Japanese: ‥‥そのために使えるモノは、 なんだって使うわ。
English: The end justifies the means, Mr. Phoenix Wright. The end justifies the means.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣。 今日の法廷はなんだ？
English: What happened today at the trial, Edgeworth? That was not like you at all...

Japanese: お前だって、霧緒さんの ことは知ってるはずだ。
English: I mean, I know you knew about Ms. Andrews' condition.

Japanese: いくら証言させるためとはいえ、 何もあそこまで‥‥！
English: You could have made her testify as many times as you wanted, but to go that far...!

Japanese: ‥‥ああでも言わなければ、 彼女は証言しなかった。
English: ...Ah, but she wouldn't testify about THAT until I said something.

Japanese: いいか、成歩堂。 法廷は、人間を裁く庭だ。
English: Listen, Wright. The courtroom is a garden of judgment.

Japanese: 私は、イノチをかけて あの場所に立っている。
English: I am putting myself on the line when I stand in there.

Japanese: だから、証人にもそれを求める。 ‥‥当然のことだろう。
English: And that's why I made the witness do the same... It's only natural.

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、御剣‥‥
English: By the way, Edgeworth...

Japanese: 今日の法廷‥‥めずらしく うろたえていたよな？
English: You were really angry in court today... That's rare for you.

Japanese: 証人ッ！　そのカード‥‥ こちらにわたすのだッ！　早く！
English: Witness! That card... Give it to me! Hurry!

Japanese: キサマは‥‥なんという、 バカなことをしてくれたのだ！
English: Do you have any idea what you have stupidly, yet inadvertently done!?

Japanese: コイツを‥‥今まで 私の目からかくしておくなんて！
English: This... I can't believe you hid this from me all this time!

Japanese: あのカード‥‥ いったい、なんだったんだ？
English: That card... What in the world is it?

Japanese: コイツのことか‥‥
English: You mean this...?

Japanese: いいか。これはゴクヒ情報だ。 ‥‥だれにももらすな。
English: Listen, Wright. This is top secret information. You absolutely cannot leak this.

Japanese: 特別捜査課が何年も追っているが、 署内でも、知らない人間が多い。
English: A special investigations team has existed for a number of years, but few know of it.

Japanese: わ、わかった‥‥。
English: I-I understand.

Japanese: このカードの持ち主‥‥
English: Their task is to find the "owner" of this card...

Japanese: 名前は、虎狼死家　左々右エ門 （ころしやさざえもん）という。
English: A man called Shelly de Killer.

Japanese: その名のとおり、殺し屋だ。 ‥‥スゴウデの、な。
English: And just as his name states, he is a "killer", an assassin. The best, at that.
(An assassin!?)

Japanese: 証拠品《カード》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Picture Card added to the Court Record.

Japanese: コロシヤ　サザエモン ‥‥って‥‥？
English: So who is this "Shelly de Killer"?

Japanese: “虎狼死家”は、屋号だ。 ‥‥老舗のな。
English: "De Killer" is the name of a long standing line of assassins.
Long standing?

Japanese: 初代が現れたのは、 もう１００年も前のことらしい。
English: The name first appeared about 100 years ago, I hear.

Japanese: 左々右エ門は、虎狼死家の ３代目を襲名した殺し屋だ。
English: Shelly is the professional name of the third heir to the De Killer name.

Japanese: “左々右エ門”だから、 サザエのカードを‥‥？
English: So because his professional name is "Shelly", he leaves cards with a shell on them...?

Japanese: ヤツは、殺したエモノのそばに、 かならずカードを残していく。
English: He has a habit of making sure to leave a card by the body of his victims.

Japanese: どうして、そんなことを‥‥
English: Why would he do something like that...?

Japanese: 依頼人に対する義理、 と考えられている。
English: We think it is a part of his duty to his clients.
His "duty"?

Japanese: カードを残すことで 自らの犯行を明らかにするわけだ。
English: If he leaves a card, then his clients can be assured it was he who killed the victim.

Japanese: 依頼人に殺人容疑が かからないようにするために。
English: It also serves as insurance against any charges being pushed onto his clients.
...I see...

Japanese: 虎狼死家は、依頼人との 信頼関係を何よりも重んじる。
English: De Killer values the trust between his clients and himself above all else.

Japanese: ‥‥義理人情に生きる 殺し屋、ということらしい。
English: It seems that this is one honorable assassin with a moral conscience.

Japanese: （‥‥殺し屋に 義理も人情もあるかよ‥‥）
English: (I guess that even honorable assassins can exist...)

Japanese: それで‥‥その殺し屋が、 この事件に‥‥？
English: So, you think this assassin... You think he's the one who did the killing in this case?

Japanese: そのようだな。
English: It would appear that way.

Japanese: このカードが発見された以上、 まちがいないだろう‥‥。
English: The discovery of the card basically confirms it, wouldn't you agree?

Japanese: （虎狼死家　左々右エ門‥‥）
English: (Shelly de Killer, huh...)

Japanese: 今日の法廷‥‥じつは、 私も気になっていた。
English: I noticed something at the trial today.

Japanese: キミも、妙にうろたえていた。 ‥‥何かあったのか？
English: You were behaving in a very strange manner. ...Is something the matter?

Japanese: （こいつには‥‥話しておく べきかもしれない）
English: (I guess... I should just tell him.)

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんが‥‥誘拐されたんだ。
English: Maya... She's been kidnapped.

Japanese: ゆ‥‥誘拐だと‥‥？ 犯人の要求は！
English: K-Kidnapped? What does the kidnapper want!?

Japanese: 無罪判決だ。
English: An acquittal.

Japanese: ‥‥そういうことだったのか。
English: I see. I had no idea.

Japanese: 大至急、捜査班を編成しよう。 明日までに、解決する。
English: I will prepare a rescue team as soon as possible, and resolve this by tomorrow.

Japanese: ほ‥‥ホントですかッ！
English: R-Really!?

Japanese: なるほどくん！ み、ミツルギ検事が‥‥
English: Did you hear that, Mr. Nick!? M-Mr. Edgeworth is going to...

Japanese: 気休めはよしてくれ！
English: Stop trying to console me, Edgeworth! I don't need your pity!

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥？
English: Mr. Nick?

Japanese: 見つかるはずがない。 手がかりが、まったくないんだ！
English: There's no way you can find her. We don't have even a single clue to go on.

Japanese: 彼女を助ける方法は、 １つしかない。
English: There's only one way to save her.

Japanese: ‥‥なんとしてでも、無罪判決を モギ取るしかないんだよ！
English: I... I have to get an acquittal somehow! It's the only way!

Japanese: 成歩堂。これだけは言っておく。
English: Wright. Listen, you need to know something.

Japanese: 藤見野　イサオを殺害したのは、 虎狼死家　左々右エ門だ。
English: Juan Corrida was killed by Shelly de Killer.

Japanese: そして、その殺人を 依頼したのは‥‥
English: And the client who ordered the job...

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟‥‥ キサマの依頼人だ。
English: is Matt Engarde... Your own client.

Japanese: やめてくれ！ ‥‥信じるわけにはいかない。
English: Please, stop! ...I can't listen to you. I can't believe that.

Japanese: ‥‥わかった。
English: ...I see.

Japanese: キミは捜査をつづけたまえ。 ‥‥これを持って行くといい。
English: Well, if you want to continue your investigation, you will need this.

Japanese: これは‥‥？
English: What is it?

Japanese: ホテルは現在、 立入禁止になっている。
English: The hotel right now is restricted to police personnel only,

Japanese: 虎狼死家　左々右エ門の 手がかりを探すためにな。
English: as we are looking for any clues that might lead us to Shelly de Killer.

Japanese: ‥‥それを持っていけば、 入れてもらえるだろう。
English: If you take this with you to the hotel, I'm sure they will let you enter.

Japanese: 《御剣の紹介状》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Letter of Introduction added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、真宵くんの 手配をしておこう。
English: ...In any case, I must attend to the preparations for Maya's rescue team.

Japanese: ‥‥シツレイする。 何かあったら、また会おう。
English: We'll meet again, if anything should happen. Now if you'll excuse me...

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥
English: Mr. Nick?

Japanese: まさか‥‥まさか、 オートロさんが、殺し屋さんを‥‥
English: Do you... Do you think... Mr. Engarde hired an assassin...?

Japanese: そんなハズはないよ！
English: No way!

Japanese: （だって‥‥彼には、 サイコ・ロックはなかった‥‥）
English: (I mean, he doesn't have a Psyche-Lock...)

Japanese: そ、そうですよね‥‥。
English: Y-Yeah, I guess not...

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃん‥‥
English: Maya...

Japanese: ‥‥たのむから、 ブジでいてくれよ‥‥！
English: Please... All I ask is you make it home, safe and sound!

Japanese: うわッ！
English: Aah!

Japanese: （‥‥これだけか‥‥）
English: (I guess that's all I get...)

Japanese: ‥‥私は、この事件の 担当検事だ。
English: ...I've been put in charge of this case.

Japanese: 手がかりについて 話し合うことはできない。
English: So I can't give you any help with regards to clues or evidence.

Japanese: カタいヤツだな。 イトノコ刑事を見ならえよ。
English: You sure are hardnosed. Totally different from Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: ‥‥だからヤツは クビになったのだ。
English: ...And that is why he was fired.

Japanese: （ちょっと、説得力あるな‥‥）
English: (He's got a point...)
Date: ???  Time: ??? Location: ???

Japanese: ふふん。ユーカイ犯も、 ちょっとドジだよねー。
English: Heh heh. I guess even kidnappers can be a little clumsy.

Japanese: こんなカード、 落としとくんだもん。
English: Clumsy enough to drop a card like this for me.

Japanese: “殺し屋だー”とか 言ってたけど、
English: And even though he said he was an "assassin",

Japanese: きっと、なるほどくんみたいに ハッタリだよね‥‥。
English: I bet he's just making that up, like how Nick does with everything in court.

Japanese: とりあえず、このカードで‥‥
English: Anyway, let's try out the card trick with this card I just found...

Japanese: ‥カチ‥
‥‥はずれたみたいだね、 ドアのカギ‥‥。
English: Sounds like I got the door open...

Japanese: よし！　思いきって 行ってみよう‥‥！
English: OK! Time to go take a look around!

Japanese: 暗くてよく見えないけど、 何もなさそうだね‥‥。
English: I can't see very well in this darkness, but it doesn't seem like there's anything there...

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、ごちゃごちゃ つみあげられてるけど‥‥
English: There's all sorts of things piled up here...

Japanese: 暗くてよく見えないな。
English: But it's too dark to see.

Japanese: なんだろ、これ。 ビンがいっぱいあるよ。
English: What's this...? It feels like there are a lot of glass bottles here.

Japanese: それに‥‥タルだねこりゃ。
English: And these... They feel like barrels.

Japanese: うう‥‥おなかすいたし、 いただいちゃおうかなあ。
English: I'll pass... Too bad I'm really hungry, and not really thirsty.

Japanese: カギはこのカードで はずしたし‥‥
English: I've unlocked the door with this card...

Japanese: ちょっと、冒険してみても いいよね‥‥。
English: I should probably go and take a look around...
Date: ???  Time: ??? Location: ???

Japanese: うわ‥‥。なんだろ、ここ。
English: Wh-What is this place...?

Japanese: やっぱり、あのホテルじゃ なかったみたいだね‥‥
English: I've got a feeling I'm not in the hotel anymore...

Japanese: あそこにあるの、ビデオかな？
English: Are those videos over there?

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、何か手がかりを 探さないと。
English: ...Well, I'll worry about that later. For now, I should be looking for clues.

Japanese: それで‥‥お姉ちゃんに 見てもらうんだ！
English: That way, I can show them to sis and maybe get out of here!

Japanese: 困りますね‥‥ 勝手に出歩かれては。
English: Oh, this simply will not do... I cannot have you wandering around at will.

Japanese: きゃわわあああああああああっ！
English: Eeeeeaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら‥‥
English: ...It seems...

Japanese: あなたの弁護士さんは、 シッパイしたようですねえ。
English: that your Mr. Wright is truly concerned about you.

Japanese: な、なるほどくんが‥‥？
English: He is...?

Japanese: とにかく、あなたは おとなしくしていることです。
English: For now, I would suggest you remain cooperative.

Japanese: できないのであれば、 私がお手伝いしますよ‥‥？
English: If you cannot, there are ways in which I can help you...

Japanese: そ、それって‥‥
English: Ways... You mean...

Japanese: “死人に口なし”と 申しますでしょう‥‥？
English: "Dead men tell no tales" is how the saying goes, correct?

Japanese: ししししししし、しにん！
English: "D-D-D-D-Dead"!?

Japanese: 最初に申したはずです。 私は‥‥殺し屋だと。
English: I'm almost certain I told you on our first meeting. I am... an assassin.

Japanese: う‥‥ウソ‥‥ ころしや、なんて‥‥
English: N-No way... You're lying... I mean... An assassin...?

Japanese: ‥‥人は見かけによらない、 と申しますよ。
English: ...People are not always who they appear to be.

Japanese: なるほどくうううううぅぅぅぅん！
English: Niiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiick!

Japanese: わあ。こんな大きなテレビ、 見たことないよ。
English: Wow, I've never seen a TV this big before.

Japanese: スイッチ、スイッチ‥‥
English: Now where's the power button... Hmm...

Japanese: ‥カチ‥
ちぇ。つかないや。
English: Phooey. It's busted.

Japanese: でも、こんなテレビで トノサマンが見られたら、
English: I would so die a happy Samurai fan...

Japanese: あたしもう、死んでもいいや。
English: if I ever got to see the Nickel Samurai on a TV like this.

Japanese: いやいやいや！ シャレにならないな、今のは。
English: Ack! I can't believe I just made a joke about dying, all things considered...

Japanese: なんだろ、これ。 アンテナ‥‥かな？
English: What is this thing? An antenna... I guess?

Japanese: それと‥‥ビデオデッキか。
English: And this is... a VCR?

Japanese: ずいぶんいろんなキカイが あるなあ。
English: There sure are a lot of electronic gadgets here.

Japanese: でも‥‥なんでこんなところに アンテナがあるんだろ‥‥？
English: But what is an antenna doing here...?

Japanese: お。パソコンだよ。
English: Oh, hey. It's a computer.

Japanese: あたし、さわったこと ないんだよねー。
English: I've never really used one before.

Japanese: で‥‥電源スイッチが どこだか、わかんない。
English: Um... I have no idea where the power switch on this thing is...

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっとショック。
English: Drat. There goes my plan to use this somehow to get out of here.

Japanese: ううう‥‥やっぱり カギがかかってるよ。
English: Unngh... Locked. Of course.

Japanese: こりゃ、カードでカチ、 ってわけにはいかないなあ。
English: And it doesn't look like I can use the card to open this door.

Japanese: ‥‥ドアの下に、小さな 入り口がついている。
English: There's a little hole at the bottom of the door.

Japanese: もうちょっとヤセてたら、 出られたかもしれないな‥‥。
English: If only I was a little skinnier. Then maybe I'd be able to crawl through there...

Japanese: 暗くてよくわかんないけど‥‥
English: It's so dark in here that I can barely see, but...

Japanese: ビデオカセット‥‥みたいだね、 ぜーんぶ。
English: these kinda feel like... video tapes... All of them...

Japanese: なんの部屋なのかなあ‥‥ここ。
English: Just what kind of room is this...?

Japanese: ‥‥なんだろ。ソファに アクセサリーが置いてあるよ‥‥
English: ...That's weird. What's a figurine doing on a sofa in a place like this...?

Japanese: クマさん‥‥だね、これ。 かーわいい！
English: I think it's... a bear. Aww, how cute!

Japanese: でも、このクマさん‥‥ 切れ目がいっぱい入ってるよ。
English: But it's got a lot of cuts and slits on it...

Japanese: ‥‥パズル‥‥なのかな。
English: I wonder if it's some kind of puzzle or something...

Japanese: 写真立てがある。
English: There's a framed picture sitting on this coffee table.

Japanese: これ‥‥女のヒトだね。 ちょっとキレイだな。
English: It's a picture of a woman... She's kind of pretty.

Japanese: お。なんか書いてある。 ‥‥なになに。
English: Hey, looks like something's written here... Let's see...

Japanese: “愛をこめて‥‥ユリエ” ‥‥かあ。
English: I think it says, "With love... Celeste"

Japanese: これはひょっとして、 手がかりになるかもね‥‥
English: I bet this could be a clue...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 王都楼邸　応接間
English: March 22 Engarde Mansion Living Room

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥すっかり 暗くなっちまったな‥‥）
English: (Hmm, sure is dark...)

Japanese: じゃあ、電気をつけますね。
English: I'll go turn on a light!

Japanese: はあ‥‥。 さすが、スターの家だな。
English: Wow... So this is what a star's house looks like. Must be nice to be rich.

Japanese: さあ。シュウちゃんを 探しましょう。
English: Come on, Mr. Nick! Let's find Shoe, the kitty-cat!

Japanese: シュウちゃあん！
English: Shoooooe!

Japanese: にゃあん。
English: Meow.

Japanese: お。コイツか。
English: So I guess this is Shoe.

Japanese: まあ。愛らしいネコ。 ‥‥シュウちゃん。
English: Ah, what a lovely cat. ...Hello, Shoe.

Japanese: にゃあん。
English: Meow.

Japanese: ‥‥ふふ。
English: ...Tee hee.

Japanese: （気に入ったみたいだ）
English: (The cat seems to like Pearls.)

Japanese: すみません‥‥。
English: Pardon me...

Japanese: シツレイですが、 あなたさまは‥‥？
English: May I help you with something, Mister...

Japanese: あ。あの、ぼくたちは弁護士です。 ‥‥王都楼さんの。
English: Oh, uh, we're lawyers. Actually, I'm Mr. Engarde's lawyer.

Japanese: ダンナさまの‥‥。
English: The master's...

Japanese: では‥‥あなたが、成歩堂さま。
English: Then, you must be Mr. Wright.

Japanese: は、はい。
English: Y-Yes.

Japanese: これはお見それしました。
English: Ah, it's a pleasure to meet your wonderful self.

Japanese: 私、この家の執事で 田中　太郎と申します。
English: I am the family butler, John Doe.

Japanese: どうも‥‥ よろしくおねがいします。
English: Nice to meet you.

Japanese: にゃあん。
English: Meow.

Japanese: ‥‥では。
English: ...Well,

Japanese: 私はこのへんで シツレイさせていただきます。
English: I'm afraid I must take my leave of you now.

Japanese: あ、ぼくたちも帰ります。
English: Oh, we should probably get going ourselves.

Japanese: おワカいのに、たいしたものです。 弁護士、とは‥‥。
English: Ah, so young and yet already so accomplished. A master of law...

Japanese: あなたのほうは、執事のカンロク じゅうぶんですね。
English: But there is also a lot to be proud of in being a butler, in charge of the house and all.

Japanese: そんなことはございません。
English: Thank you for the compliment, sir.

Japanese: ‥‥人は見かけによらない、 と申しますよ。
English: ...People are not always who they appear to be.

Japanese: ‥‥では。
English: ...Now if you'll excuse me.

Japanese: にゃあん。
English: Meow.

Japanese: きょっ、巨大な自転車が 空を飛んでいます！
English: A... A giant bicycle is flying through the air!

Japanese: あの自転車はね。 こがなくても走るんだよ。
English: That bicycle, Pearls, is one where you don't have to pedal, and it moves on its own.

Japanese: ええッ！　すごい！
English: Really!? Wow!

Japanese: でもね。 ザンネンながら、飛ばないんだ。
English: But sorry to disappoint you, it can't fly.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ。ガッカリです。
English: ...Oh. That's too bad.

Japanese: あ。大きなカマドがあります。
English: Oh! There's a giant cooking hearth here.

Japanese: それはダンロだよ。
English: That's actually a fireplace.

Japanese: どうちがうのですか？
English: How are they different, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: （そういえば、カマドって 見たことないな、ぼく‥‥）
English: (You know, I've never actually seen a hearth before, come to think of it...)

Japanese: 綾里のお屋敷に、遊びに 来てください。お見せしますから。
English: You should come and visit Fey Manor, then. I'll show you one when you do.

Japanese: ゆったりとした 応接セットだ。
English: It's a very comfortable and spacious lounge set.

Japanese: 有名スターたちが 笑いさざめくのだろうか。
English: I wonder if famous stars drop by and sit around and have a good time.

Japanese: ‥‥どうせ、ぼくの 居場所なんか、ないだろうな。
English: ...In any case, I don't really belong here, do I?

Japanese: なんだ？ ‥‥このヒガミ根性は。
English: Argh. What is with me and feeling inferior today...?

Japanese: あ。お面があります！
English: Ah! There are masks here!

Japanese: ああ。まんなかのマスクが トノサマンだよ。
English: Yeah. That one in the middle is the Steel Samurai.

Japanese: あとは、ヒメサマンと、 アクダイカーン。
English: The ones next to that are the Pink Princess and the Evil Magistrate.

Japanese: ネオ・エドシティを舞台に、 いつも戦っているんだ。
English: They fought many battles against the backdrop of Neo Olde Tokyo.

Japanese: はあ‥‥なるほどくん、 お詳しいのですね。
English: Wow, you really know a lot about the Steel Samurai, Mr. Nick.

Japanese: （子どもより詳しいと、 なんか恥ずかしいな‥‥）
English: (I don't know whether to laugh or cry that I know more about that show than a kid...)

Japanese: ドアの下に、ちっちゃな ドアがついています。
English: There's a small door at the bottom of this bigger door, Mr. Nick.

Japanese: ネコが出入りするための ものじゃないかな。
English: I bet it's for Mr. Engarde's cat to use.

Japanese: ああ。シュウちゃん。
English: Oh, you mean Shoe.

Japanese: （ドアには‥‥ カギがかかっている）
English: (The door... It's locked tight.)

Japanese: （まあ、勝手に出入りしちゃ マズいよな‥‥）
English: (Well, I guess that's to keep nosey people like me from entering it.)

Japanese: あそこにもドアがある。
English: There's another door over there.

Japanese: 行ってはダメですよ、 なるほどくん。
English: You shouldn't go wandering off over there, Mr. Nick.

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: ...Yes, Pearls.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんのときと、 立場が逆だな‥‥）
English: (Now I know how Maya feels when I tell her to stop playing around...)

Japanese: 王都楼さんのこと‥‥ もちろん、ご存じですよね？
English: You must know all sorts of things about Mr. Engarde, right?

Japanese: まったく‥‥とんでもない ゴカイだと思います。
English: Honestly, sir... I don't believe my master is capable of such a foul deed as murder.

Japanese: あの。他には、何か？
English: And, uh, anything else?

Japanese: 特にございません。
English: No, not especially.

Japanese: 主人のことを話すのは、 使用人にふさわしくありません。
English: It is not appropriate for a lowly servant to speak of the master or his affairs.

Japanese: （ううん。“執事さん”って 感じだな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... How typically "butler-like", as it were...)

Japanese: 田中さん、 この家にはどれくらい‥‥？
English: Mr. Doe, how long have you served at this residence...?

Japanese: そうでございますね。 ‥‥１年ほどになりますか。
English: Well, sir, I would have to say... maybe about one year?

Japanese: あの。他には、何か？
English: And, uh, anything else?

Japanese: 特にございません。
English: No, not especially.

Japanese: 自分のことを話すのは、 使用人にふさわしくありません。
English: It is not appropriate for a lowly servant to speak of himself and his affairs.

Japanese: （王都楼さんには、“メイドさん” というイメージがあるな‥‥）
English: (You know, I would've thought Mr. Engarde the kind to have a "maid" over a butler...)

Japanese: かわいいネコですね。
English: That's a very cute cat you've got here.

Japanese: 私が世話をしておりますよ。 主人のお気に入りです。
English: It is my duty to take care of him. The master rather fancies Shoe.

Japanese: あの。他には、何か？
English: And, uh, anything else?

Japanese: 特にございません。
English: No, not especially.

Japanese: ネコのことを話すのは、 使用人にふさわしくありません。
English: It is not appropriate for a lowly servant to speak of the family cat.

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ。この紙きれ、 もう、いらないな‥‥）
English: (...Well then. I guess I don't need this piece of scrap paper anymore.)

Japanese: 《王都楼のメモ》を まるめて捨てた。
English: Matt's Note crumpled into a ball and thrown away.

Japanese: ‥‥これ、見て いただきたいんですけど。
English: I was wondering if you wouldn't mind taking a look at this.

Japanese: 特に申しあげることは ございませんね。
English: I'm afraid I cannot offer up anything special about it.

Japanese: 証拠品のことを話すのは、 使用人にふさわしくありません。
English: It is not appropriate for a lowly servant to speak of evidence for a trial.

Japanese: （インギン無礼。‥‥執事さんの イメージにピッタリだな）
English: (Polite yet snotty with a touch of rude. He's the stereotypical butler alright.)

Japanese: この人物なんですけど‥‥
English: Would you happen to know who this person is...?

Japanese: 存じあげません。
English: No, I would not.

Japanese: 主人の知人について話すのは、 使用人にふさわしくありません。
English: It is not appropriate for a lowly servant to speak of the master's acquaintances.

Japanese: （じゃあアンタは、いったい 何をしゃべればイイんだよ！）
English: (Then what in the world CAN I talk to you about!?)

Japanese: な、なるほどくん。 怒ってはダメです！
English: M-Mr. Nick! Being angry is bad!
